chapterbody: n October 2011, I had expounded the dharma in both Seattle Ling Shen Ching Tze Temple and Rainbow Temple in the United States for a duration of two months. During these two months, the dharmas that I taught included numerous essential and precious pith instructions.
chapterbody 3: In addition:
chapterbody 5: At the “Chang Hung Temple” of Panama, I expounded the mnemonic of the “Nine-Syllable Mantra Syllable-Cutting Formula” and “Kalachakra’s Sun-Moon Disc Practice,” which were very hard to come by.
chapterbody 7: At the “St. Dak Temple” in the state of Florida of the United States, I transmitted the secret among secrets, the “Supreme Divine Water Practice.”
chapterbody 9: At the “Lian Sheng Temple” in the city of Philadelphia of the United States, I taught “The Padmakumara Dharma Lineage Flow,” which was deeply unfathomable.
chapterbody 11: At the “True Buddha Diamond Temple of New York” in the United States, I preached “The Sadhana of Mahapratisara Bodhisattva,” which was really rare and precious.
chapterbody 13: At the “Houston True Buddha Temple” in the state of Texas of the United States, I transmitted “Kalachakra’s Five Wheels” and explained “Nirvana is Joy.” These were hardly heard of in this world. Etc…
chapterbody 15: The pith instructions in these teachings and the practice of diligent cultivation originated from my lineage masters to whom I pay homage to:
chapterbody 17: Reverend Liaoming.
chapterbody 18: His Holiness the Sixteenth Dharma King (Karmapa).
chapterbody 19: Vajra Master Sakya Zhengkong.
chapterbody 20: Vajra Master Thubten Dargye.
chapterbody 21: And all Masters.
chapterbody 23: My cultivation is just like my writing, in that it has not ever been interrupted in the past forty years. In fact, in this current life, it is difficult to find anyone with such perseverance and fortitude towards meditative practice. Therefore, my blessing [to sentient beings] is limitless and so is my achievement.
chapterbody 25: I also predicted:
chapterbody 27: 1. Attainment of Buddhahood in this current life.
chapterbody 28: 2. Rebirth in the pure land.
chapterbody 29: 3. Eternally living in this world and turning the dharma wheel.
chapterbody 31: In this life, I am a cultivator who has set the foundation in benefiting the generations of cultivators to come. This is my wish and is also the vow that I made.
chapterbody 33: Over forty years ago, I was at the “Jade Emperor Temple” of Taichung where I coincidentally met “Madame Lin Qiandai” and was guided by her. Golden Mother of the Jade Pond descended and opened my Divine Eyes.
chapterbody 35: Golden Mother of the Jade Pond said, “I have been looking for you for a long time!”
chapterbody 37: And said, “I have helped to transfer your [military] posting so that you will be permanently stationed in Taichung.” (Referring to my life in the army.)
chapterbody 39: And said, “A great opportunity will arise and a Great Dharma will be passed on to you.”
chapterbody 41: And said, “Follow the buddhas wholeheartedly, practice wholeheartedly, and perform good deeds wholeheartedly.”
chapterbody 42: (I was twenty-six years old at that time.)
chapterbody 44: Starting from that day, I encountered miracles almost every day to the extent that I could not finish recounting all of them even if I were to do so.
chapterbody 46: I flew to the buddha land of Padmasambhava.
chapterbody 47: I visited ancient tombs or caves.
chapterbody 48: I was swallowed through the mouth of Padmasambhava, and then reborn from Padmasambhava.
chapterbody 49: Padmasambhava transformed into rainbow light. I merged into the rainbow light and bathed in all the empowerments from the lineage of Padmasambhava; the flow of his lineage empowerments diffused through my eighty-four thousand pores.
chapterbody 51: I experienced:
chapterbody 52: The splitting of the sky.
chapterbody 53: The shaking of the mountains, rivers, and lands.
chapterbody 54: I wrote my experiences, but was misconstrued by people as mad.
chapterbody 55: (Therein lies the secret of the awakening of my faith)
chapterbody 56: I wish this book, [Open Your Mind], can make people generate faith in practicing Buddhism.
chapterbody 57: Living Buddha Lian-sheng
chapterbody 58: Sheng-yen Lu

chapterbody 61: U.S.A.

chapterbody: may look a little crazy, but this craziness is hardly enough for admittance to the mental hospital.
chapterbody 2: In fact, there are also ghosts at “True Buddha Quarter,” but these ghosts are my disciples who took refuge in me.
chapterbody 3: My reasons for “craziness” are:
chapterbody 5: Being friends with ghosts.
chapterbody 6: Being friends with gods.
chapterbody 7: Being friends with bodhisattvas.
chapterbody 8: Being friends with buddhas.
chapterbody 10: These reasons may be enough to cause people to classify me as a going-to-be-crazy person, because similar types of people have been locked up in the mental hospital.
chapterbody 12: There is a joke [about mental patients]:
chapterbody 14: The Dean of a mental hospital asked a patient, “Who told you that you are Jesus?”
chapterbody 16: The patient answered, “God.”
chapterbody 18: Another patient who passed by then told the patient who claimed to be Jesus, “I did not tell you that you are Jesus.”
chapterbody 20: After reading this joke, I had a big laugh.
chapterbody 22: Therefore, when people call me a madman upon knowing my situation, I cannot blame them because I am not very different from a madman.
chapterbody 24: I am otherwise a normal person.
chapterbody 25: When it is time to sleep, I sleep.
chapterbody 26: When it is time to cultivate, I cultivate.
chapterbody 27: When it is time to write, I write.
chapterbody 28: When it is time to paint, I paint.
chapterbody 30: I do not wear thick underwear in the summer, and I definitely do not wear just a t-shirt in the winter. I put on a scarf when I feel cold, and a thin robe when I feel warm.
chapterbody 32: I have a normal life.
chapterbody 33: I neither break laws nor violate precepts, and I lead a disciplined life.
chapterbody 34: I have breakfast at 6 o’clock in the morning, lunch at noon time, and dinner at 6 o’clock in the evening.
chapterbody 35: I socialize with people normally.
chapterbody 37: I said: I am not a saint, I am not great, I am just an ordinary person. Everything runs naturally, and being natural is the path (the Tao). Laozi said: “Tao follows the laws of nature.”
chapterbody 39: I think: “Saints must be the most unwise people in the world.” (Self-binding)
chapterbody 41: I understand the philosophy of the “Book of Changes”: “A great man’s virtue is in harmony with that of heaven and earth, his brightness is in harmony with that of the sun and the moon, his orderliness is in harmony with those of the four seasons, and his [encounters of] auspiciousness and ominousness are in harmony with those of gods and ghosts.”
chapterbody 43: 1. Observe morality.
chapterbody 44: 2. Purify body, speech, and mind.
chapterbody 45: 3. Live naturally.
chapterbody 46: 4. Understand the reasons behind auspiciousness and ominousness, and bad and good fortune.
chapterbody 48: The four items listed above are in tandem with the philosophy of the Book of Changes. Being friends with gods and ghosts is something which
chapterbody 49: I have in excess of a normal person, and therefore I am a person who is able tell the auspiciousness and ominousness of things.
chapterbody 51: I have numerous ghost friends, and they are all my good friends. One year, I was facing an imminent calamity.
chapterbody 52: A ghost told me, “A big imminent calamity.”
chapterbody 55: The ghost answered, “Escape!”
chapterbody 58: The ghost answered, “Create a diversion.” (Make people think that you are here, when in fact, you are somewhere else, ha ha ha!)
chapterbody 60: My ghost friends helped me, and my celestial friends also helped me, so that I could breeze through a disaster.
chapterbody 63: I asked, “What to do?”
chapterbody 64: I asked, “How to escape?”
chapterbody 65: I am not bragging about the friendships that I have with ghosts, gods, bodhisattvas, and buddhas, because anyone can do so. Yao and Shun were human beings, and anyone could be Yao or Shun; Shakyamuni Buddha was a human being, and you could also be Shakyamuni Buddha. On this point, I am not deceiving you!

chapterbody: have moved several times while I lived in Seattle in the state of Washington of the United States.
chapterbody 3: The first time, I moved from “Ballard” to “True Buddha Quarter.”
chapterbody 4: The second time, I moved from “True Buddha Quarter” to “Phantom Lake.”
chapterbody 5: The third time, I moved from “Phantom Lake” to “South Mountain Residence.”
chapterbody 6: The fourth time, I moved from “South Mountain Residence” to “New South Mountain Residence.”
chapterbody 8: There was a ghost at the basement of my Ballard house, and this ghost was not too friendly to Fo-ching and Fo-chi.
chapterbody 10: When I was moving out, I could even hear them sighing in the basement.
chapterbody 12: “New South Mountain Residence” is my last home and it occupies two acres of land. It is a bigger house and two ghosts live there.
chapterbody 14: Every morning when I wake up, I have to walk along a long corridor. When I pass by the guest restroom located along that corridor, a loud “KA” noise would always sound.
chapterbody 15: The same thing happens everyday.
chapterbody 17: As it turned out, the ghosts in the restroom were startled by the light emitting from me.
chapterbody 19: Initially, there was just only one ghost, but later, another ghost surprisingly moved into “New South Mountain Residence” when I happened to stay in Taiwan longer than usual.
chapterbody 21: These two ghosts are not harmful to us.
chapterbody 23: One of the ghosts would even salute me in military style, because he used to be an American soldier and he knew that I was a former Lieutenant commander. Therefore, every time he sees me, he will salute me.
chapterbody 25: When his shoes collide, they also generate a “KA” sound.
chapterbody 27: This ghost helps me a lot.
chapterbody 29: Every time I perform the transformation of nectar water for offering:
chapterbody 31: Great Peng Golden-Winged Bird
chapterbody 32: All the ghosts and spirits of the wilderness
chapterbody 33: Raksasa and Hariti
chapterbody 34: All filled up with nectar
chapterbody 35: Om, mu-di-li, so-ha. Om, mu-di-li, so-ha. Om, mu-di-li, so-ha.
chapterbody 37: This American soldier ghost helps to maintain order for those coming to receive my nectar offering.
chapterbody 39: There was once a wandering mangy ghost who wanted to stay in “New South Mountain Residence” But, as he had a bad temperament, he was considered unqualified by the American soldier ghost who pulled him out and made him leave.
chapterbody 41: In fact, there are also ghosts at “True Buddha Quarter,” but these ghosts are my disciples who took refuge in me. They are very good; they know how to cultivate Vajrayana Dharma and help me a lot. All the ghosts in “True Buddha Quarter” can fly and make supernatural transformation. They are already getting close to being “gods.”
chapterbody 42: I asked them, “Is it good being a ghost?”
chapterbody 44: They answered, “Good!”
chapterbody 46: “How is it good?” I asked.
chapterbody 48: The group of ghosts said, “A ghost has neither form nor body, just spirit. No form or body results in less sickness and affliction. We all have a little bit of supernatural power, are able to transform and fly, and can go anywhere we please. There is so much freedom and we do not want to be bounded by the physical form, which would be very troublesome.”
chapterbody 50: I said, “Ghosts may reincarnate again to become human beings.”
chapterbody 53: Once, I was going out.
chapterbody 54: A ghost said, “Remember to bring an umbrella!”
chapterbody 56: “It is really sunny, why bring umbrella?” I was puzzled.
chapterbody 57: “It will rain at three o’clock in the afternoon!” the ghost said.
chapterbody 59: Sure enough, in the afternoon, the sky turned dark.
chapterbody 61: Three o’clock in the afternoon, the rain started pouring.
chapterbody 62: The ghost had predicted accurately.
chapterbody 67: The group of ghosts answered, “Only a few reincarnate to become human beings due to affinity and karma. In fact, human beings are ghosts and ghosts are human beings; the only difference between them is that one has a physical body and the other does not have. We are happier without the physical body.”
chapterbody 69: The ghost answered, “Did you not say that ghosts have five supernatural powers?”
chapterbody 71: Let me tell you: we should all believe that ghosts, gods, bodhisattvas, and buddhas exist.

chapterbody: t is well known that Grand Master Lu makes friends with, corresponds with, communicates with, and interacts cordially with ghosts.
chapterbody 3: One day, an old female ghost leisurely arrived.
chapterbody 5: This ghost wore a stiff and blank facial expression, looked extremely lonely, and did not speak. She just pointed to her heart.
chapterbody 7: I asked, “Is something on your mind?”
chapterbody 9: She nodded.
chapterbody 11: She wanted me to go with her and also to take me with her. Actually, I was quite unwilling, but, since a skillful man is a bold man, I went ahead without much hesitation. Perhaps, her daughter is a beautiful female ghost!
chapterbody 13: We walked into a house.
chapterbody 15: She pointed at a wooden pillar underneath a round table.
chapterbody 17: She said, “Open.”
chapterbody 19: The wooden pillar separated, and in the hollow space within, there was a large stash of jewelry. My eyes lit up at the sight:
chapterbody 21: Diamond rings and diamonds.
chapterbody 22: Gold.
chapterbody 23: Pearl necklaces.
chapterbody 24: Other jewelry.
chapterbody 25: Jade bracelets.
chapterbody 27: The jewelry was in a big bag hidden in the hollow part of the table pillar, which was a perfect place for hiding the jewelry.
chapterbody 29: I asked, “Are they for me?” I was so elated.
chapterbody 31: She answered, “No! They are for my descendants!”
chapterbody 33: This time, it was my turn to wear a stiff facial expression.
chapterbody 35: After a few days, the Zhuang family came to my place and indicated their request for help:
chapterbody 37: When the old grandmother was alive, she had a large quantity of jewelry, which included gold, silver, diamonds, and pearls. After she passed away, the jewelry disappeared. When she was alive, the old grandmother had a habit of hiding things because she was afraid of being robbed or stolen from by thieves.
chapterbody 39: Her habit included hiding things in the:
chapterbody 41: “Ceiling.”
chapterbody 42: “Basement.”
chapterbody 43: “Back of the plated plaque.”
chapterbody 44: “Freezer.”
chapterbody 45: “Sofa.”
chapterbody 46: “Toilet.”
chapterbody 47: “Belly of the Maitreya Buddha Statue.”
chapterbody 48: “Bottom of the bed.”
chapterbody 49: “Pillow case.”
chapterbody 50: Etc…
chapterbody 53: I thought to myself, “They have eventually come!”
chapterbody 54: Disciple Zhuang said, “If we are able to find the jewelry, we will donate to Sheng-Yen Lu Foundation. We will definitely do it.”
chapterbody 56: I thought to myself, “That’s more like it!”
chapterbody 57: I asked, “Have you searched everywhere?”
chapterbody 59: “Yes, we have searched numerous times; all the possible hiding places from the ceiling to the basement, including the belly of the Maitreya Bodhisattva statue.”
chapterbody 61: I said, “Inside the hollow wooden pillar of the round table.”
chapterbody 62: After they went home and searched, they really found it.
chapterbody 67: Due to her sudden death, she had no time to say or explain, and none of the descendants were aware of the whereabouts of the jewelry. Hence, we especially came to seek help from Grand Master Lu to find out the exact location of old grandmother’s jewelry.
chapterbody 68: Disciple Zhuang asked me, “Grand Master Lu, how did you know?”
chapterbody 69: I said, “Your old grandmother personally came and told me.”
chapterbody 71: Let me tell you: we should all believe that ghosts, gods, bodhisattvas, and buddhas exist.

chaptername: 004 The Water God of Lake Sammamish
chapterbody: hen I was staying in “True Buddha Quarter,” I frequently visited Lake Sammamish, which is the second biggest lake in Washington State.
chapterbody 3: I once wrote a book, “The Inner World of the Lake,” that featured all kinds of stories about this lake.
chapterbody 6: At one time:
chapterbody 8: I went to the lake’s pier at the small park. At the pier, there were wooden boards floating and extending onto the lake.
chapterbody 10: I went to the very end of the pier looking at the water plants at the bottom of the lake. Above the water plants, fish swam back and forth. I always enjoy watching the fish; they are always happy, and my mind will become happy afterwards.
chapterbody 12: Suddenly, I discerned gradual changes happening before my very eyes—two big eyes staring at me from the bottom of the lake.
chapterbody 14: There were also dense eyebrows, a nose, and very thick lips.
chapterbody 16: Beard covered the whole face.
chapterbody 18: I was astonished; the first thought that came to mind was illusion.
chapterbody 20: I took a look at the trees in the park, then at the mountains opposite me, and then I looked at the bottom of the lake again.
chapterbody 22: Oh heavens! That face was still there, and the two big eyes were still staring at me and sizing me up.
chapterbody 24: Isn’t it strange to find a face in the lake?
chapterbody 26: Later, I meditated under a big tree beside the lake.
chapterbody 28: In my meditation, I saw a man walk towards me. It was the same big-bearded strange visitor from the lake. He started to ask me, “You can see me?”
chapterbody 30: I answered, “I can see you.”
chapterbody 32: He said, “I can see that your eyes are different from other people’s.
chapterbody 34: Brilliance radiates from your eyes. Are they Yin Yang Eyes?”
chapterbody 36: I said, “No, mine are Divine Eyes.”
chapterbody 38: He was obviously very glad to know someone could really see him. He was very polite and started to chat with me.
chapterbody 40: He said that during one winter, the snow was so heavy that he lost control of his car, the brakes failed, and his car skidded all the way down to the bottom of the lake.
chapterbody 42: He then became a water ghost.
chapterbody 44: He asked me, “Are you afraid of water ghosts?”
chapterbody 46: I replied, “I am not afraid since I am a cultivator.”
chapterbody 48: He had a big laugh.
chapterbody 50: He told me that after he died, he realized that there were quite a lot of water ghosts in Lake Sammamish. Since he had a sturdy physique, the other water ghosts nominated him to be their ghost king. Not long after, he became the leader of all the water ghosts in Lake Sammamish.
chapterbody 53: I said, “Indeed you also have a compassionate heart!”
chapterbody 54: He said, “There are ghosts that are harmless to people.” He continued saying, “I feel that doing this is great; continuously saving quite a number of people. Eventually the heavens became aware of this and promoted me from ghost king to water god.”
chapterbody 56: I congratulated him.
chapterbody 57: He mentioned that he could also radiate lights and fly. Even though he was a water god, he could also travel in the human world.
chapterbody 59: I asked him, “Would you still want to be a human being?”
chapterbody 61: He answered, “Human beings! There are few who save and more who harm others. I don’t want to be a human being.”
chapterbody 62: After I listened to this, I could not help but feel affected.
chapterbody 67: One year, a heavy snowfall also caused another car to skid into the lake. All the ghosts were gladly awaiting for a new ghost’s imminent arrival. However, the ghost king suddenly became merciful and commanded a group of ghosts to hold up the sinking car, open the car door, and let the person escape. They even helped him swim up to the shore, and that person miraculously survived.
chapterbody 69: The soul itself has no form. But, by attaching to an animal’s body which has form, it is then able to come back and let people witness its return.

chapterbody: here was a lady named Luo Shanan who asked me, “My father was very fond of me when he was alive. Before he died, he whispered in my ear that he would certainly come back to see me after his death. But until now, why do I still not feel anything?”
chapterbody 3: I answered, “Your father had definitely come back to see you!”
chapterbody 5: “No?”
chapterbody 7: I said, “He was perched on your shoulder, why do you say no?”
chapterbody 9: “Oh!” Miss Luo Shanan exclaimed surprisingly.
chapterbody 11: Miss Luo said, “There was a strange thing that happened at the eulogy ceremony. Halfway through the ceremony, suddenly, for no reason, a bird flew in and kept making chirping sounds. At last, it landed on my shoulder and stayed there for a long time. It was reluctant to leave. It also used its beak to affectionately peck at my collar. Was this my father coming back to see me?”
chapterbody 13: I said, “Correct!”
chapterbody 15: I told Miss Luo, “The bird was the transformation of your father. He has already fulfilled his promise of coming back to see you!”
chapterbody 17: “How could my father become a bird?”
chapterbody 19: I told Miss Luo, “The soul itself has no form. But, by attaching to an animal’s body which has form, it is then able to come back and let people witness its return.”
chapterbody 21: I told Miss Luo, “Once, I conducted a bardo deliverance ceremony for my friend. I said, ‘The bardo deliverance tonight will be very special because I have received a message that all the deceased relatives and friends will gather here tonight.’ ”
chapterbody 23: “Are there any special indications?”
chapterbody 25: “We will know when the time comes!” I said mysteriously.
chapterbody 27: In the evening, during bardo deliverance ceremony, a large group of “water ants” flew in from outside and these “water ants” could fly with a pair of large wings. It was amazing to see a flock of water ants pour in.
chapterbody 29: I said, “Do not kill the water ants!”
chapterbody 31: I said, “They are all soul-attached bodies.”
chapterbody 33: One of the “water ants” flew to the top of my sutra chanting books and stayed put. It actually was my friend that passed away recently.
chapterbody 35: I said to the “water ant,” “You can understand my words. If you are my friend, fly to the white candle and circle around three times.”
chapterbody 37: That “water ant” was really obedient. It flew to the white candle and circled around three times above it.
chapterbody 39: Of all the participants of the bardo deliverance ceremony, there was not one that was not deeply amazed at what they saw.
chapterbody 41: I told Miss Luo, in the past, Marpa, the Vajrayana Kagyu Lineage Master, reversed the sequence of worship, and this resulted in the death of one of his sons.
chapterbody 43: When his son fell down from riding a horse and died, Marpa used the magic of “The Yoga of Forceful Projection” and attached the soul of his son to the body of a big bird which was commanded to fly to India to search for an honorable couple.
chapterbody 45: Marpa’s son then reincarnated and was reborn. (This story is widely circulated in Tibetan Vajrayana.)
chapterbody 47: I said:
chapterbody 49: People die and become ghosts.
chapterbody 50: Ghosts can return.
chapterbody 51: Return on the top of the altar.
chapterbody 52: Do not rashly kill a ghost-attached animal on the altar, because the deceased person might be attaching itself to the body of the animal.
chapterbody 58: Sheng-yen Lu
chapterbody 63: After Miss Luo’s father passed away, his soul attached to the body of a little bird which flew into the mourning hall and stood on the shoulder of Miss Luo. This is the truth about souls, believe it or not?
chapterbody 64: The defects caused by suicide, even after reincarnation, will carry forward to the next life as damage is already done.

chapterbody: am not hesitant to say that my younger brother, “Lu Zhaorong,” committed suicide.
chapterbody 2: If you bet big time because of your mesmerization with money and gold, you may lose everything.
chapterbody 3: He had been coddled since he was small.
chapterbody 5: A child who is too well taken care of by the parents, would often become part of the “Strawberry Generation” when they grow up, and are very fragile physically and mentally.
chapterbody 7: In junior high school, he was lured by a younger female schoolmate to sniff “Super Glue” and lived his days in hallucination.
chapterbody 9: He did not wake up to reality even after the younger female schoolmate committed suicide.
chapterbody 11: After he graduated from vocational high school, he worked in a glass manufacturing factory, still sniffing “Super Glue.” Even though his girlfriend tried to stop him, he did not listen. Eventually they broke up.
chapterbody 13: My youngest brother, who was my only brother, suffered such a big blow that he committed suicide by taking potassium cyanide.
chapterbody 15: His throat was completely burnt. In an instant, his soul departed from his body.
chapterbody 17: At that time, I was already in the United States.
chapterbody 19: My youngest brother became a suicide ghost and followed my mother to my home in the United States. He manifested three times in one night.
chapterbody 21: 1. To show to my mother who was still alive at that time;
chapterbody 22: 2. To show to Master Lian-xiang and ask for money;
chapterbody 23: 3. To show to me to treat his “throat.”
chapterbody 25: I applied the secret practice of “Surupakaya Tathagata” with the aid of ‘qi’ to help my brother. It was really marvelous that his throat could make sound and, in spite of everything, was amazingly cured.
chapterbody 27: Once this story got out, it caused an uproar in the “ghost realm.”
chapterbody 29: Many suicide ghosts came to my place one after another to seek treatment, and my home actually became a clinic for suicide ghosts.
chapterbody 31: Those who took poison sought treatment for internal organs.
chapterbody 33: Those who jumped to their deaths sought treatment for broken limbs.
chapterbody 35: Those who hung themselves sought treatment for their throats.
chapterbody 37: Those who died by carbon monoxide poisoning sought treatment for their
chapterbody 38: brains.
chapterbody 39: Those who drowned sought treatment for their lungs. Etc…
chapterbody 41: One day, a very ugly female ghost manifested to me. Her skin was completely damaged due to self-immolation.
chapterbody 43: I said to her, “You need to find a dermatologist!”
chapterbody 45: She answered, “Treatment from the dermatologist was ineffective, so I have to seek Grand Master Lu.”
chapterbody 47: I asked, “Why is this so?”
chapterbody 49: She replied, “Mister has the practice of ‘Surupakaya Tathagata.’ Accordingly, that practice will definitely cure the burnt skin on my whole body.”
chapterbody 51: So, I blew ‘qi’ on the body of the female ghost. Every mouthful of ‘qi’ expelled resulted in the appearance of a piece of “wonderful skin.” Two pieces appeared when I blew twice.
chapterbody 52: When I blew ‘qi’ onto her nipples, her face was quite embarrassed.
chapterbody 55: The three areas were completely blown at.
chapterbody 58: Her beauty is sufficient to turn my life as a cultivator upside down!
chapterbody 59: That beautiful-as-a-fairy female ghost, at the end, surprisingly made a vow to become my attendant. Compared to this refined fairy-like female ghost, all the beautiful girls of the human world are nothing but coarse.
chapterbody 61: Fortunately, I have practiced “White Skeleton Visualization.”
chapterbody 62: Otherwise, I might have taken the female ghost to be my wife!
chapterbody 63: When I blew ‘qi’ onto her secret parts, her face also showed embarrassment.
chapterbody 64: Once I finished blowing ‘qi,’ wow! Surprisingly, this ugly female ghost became as beautiful as a heavenly fairy.
chapterbody 68: Frankly speaking, I like ghosts more than people. I have many ghost disciples and they also belong to the group who “chases after the Buddha.” They help me in the invisible world, and sometimes, their help is even better than the tangible world’s.
chapterbody 69: I can communicate with the ghosts in the ghost realm.
chapterbody 70: The ghosts also know that there is a Grand Master Lu.
chapterbody 71: Here, I still want to remind people not to commit suicide because the karmic hindrance will be much heavier after committing suicide. The defects caused by suicide, even after reincarnation, will carry forward to the next life as damage is already done.

chapterbody: or people who play mahjong, most of them will have experienced some strange occurrences. After that, they will usually say: “Ghost in the cards!”
chapterbody 3: Actually, playing mahjong requires four people. The game will not work if one player is missing. Everyone thinks that four people are playing in a mahjong session, but from my observation, there are eight people playing and four of them are ghosts. Possibly, there are even more ghosts standing on the side watching the game.
chapterbody 5: There is also another experience that will make you never forget.
chapterbody 7: New mahjong learners obviously do not know how to play and they usually play without using tactics. But, the strange thing is that new learners always win. They usually make mahjong experts scream in exasperation.
chapterbody 9: Why do new learners win?
chapterbody 11: It is simple. With the ghosts’ assistance, a newcomer is being dragged into the sea of gambling and he or she becomes addicted to gambling. Then, everybody can play together!
chapterbody 13: I can see that all worldly people have the gambling nature in them. As for ghosts, they like gambling even more.
chapterbody 15: Once, I visited a casino in Southeast Asia. When I entered the casino, a bunch of ghosts screamed, “The Ghost King is here, everyone dash away!”
chapterbody 17: The bunch of ghosts really dispersed.
chapterbody 19: I grabbed a ghost and asked, “Why do you call me the Ghost King?”
chapterbody 21: The ghost answered, “The Smiling Face Ghost King is hidden at the back of your right thumb. Once he appears, we cannot compete, and you will win!”
chapterbody 23: I said, “I did not come here to gamble.”
chapterbody 25: At this time, the group of ghosts gradually returned.
chapterbody 27: I asked, “Why are you gathering in the casino?”
chapterbody 29: They answered, “We work under a Voodoo master who practices some techniques to make our casino dealer win. The majority of the casinos practice the ghost technique to make customers totally lose. We take the cards and move them around in order to make the customers lose and the dealer win.”
chapterbody 31: I had a big laugh after hearing that.
chapterbody 33: There was another occasion when I was in a Las Vegas casino to watch “xiu” [shows].
chapterbody 35: Unexpectedly, the Four Aunties decided to gamble.
chapterbody 37: At the gambling table, I was standing behind them chanting a mantra:
chapterbody 38: The Vajrakilaya Mantra:
chapterbody 40: “Om, bie-zha, ji-li-ji-la-ya, sha-er-wa, bi-ga-nian, bang, hum-pei.”
chapterbody 42: And also Venerable Xiongtian.
chapterbody 44: Both of them are super-powerful ghost kings.
chapterbody 46: They usually scare the gambling ghosts to the extent that they flee everywhere.
chapterbody 48: The Four Aunties: Chen Zhuanfang, Wei Siyan, Jiang Guanrong, and Sun Aizhen, won on every bet. The casino dealer had to consecutively change the card distributor four times.
chapterbody 50: The manager of the casino rushed there.
chapterbody 53: Every time the cards were distributed, miracles happened one after another. In every bet, the dealer was miserably defeated.
chapterbody 54: It caused a big uproar in the casino.
chapterbody 56: The opponent was at their wit’s end. (In fact, it was not because we had our good luck in the cards. It was all due to the assistance we received from the two powerful ghost kings who were manipulating the cards. The gambling ghosts collapsed to the ground. We did not lose even on one bet. The casino dealer could only laugh sadly. Again and again, they could not do anything other than to keep changing the card distributors, but the result was always the same—loss.)
chapterbody 57: Whenever I, the ancestor of the ghost king, am present, all the small ghosts must obey.
chapterbody 59: I will win.
chapterbody 61: I said, most of the casinos in Southeast Asia hire skilled masters to rear ghosts for practicing the ‘Five Ghosts Transporting Technique to make the gamblers lose.
chapterbody 62: Even Western casinos have people rearing ghosts.
chapterbody 67: The security personnel rushed there.
chapterbody 68: Therefore, if you are not skillful enough, never go to casinos. If you bet big time because of your mesmerization with money and gold, you may lose everything. Please be extra careful, everyone! The best thing is not to gamble at all.
chapterbody 70: I only saw the Three Saints of the Western Paradise appear in the sky, and then delivered my relative to the Western Heaven.

chaptername: 008 Ghost Relatives in a Large Hospital
chapterbody: very time I go to a large hospital, the ghosts I see are really like those in the farmers’ market; they are continuously walking to and fro.
chapterbody 2: There are all kinds of ghosts in the hospital. There are children, as well as old, strong, young, male, and female ghosts. There are also ghosts in “irregular forms.”
chapterbody 4: Each time I see numerous ghosts standing by a patient’s bedside or outside a patient’s room, I know that the patient’s time is up!
chapterbody 6: Once, I pointed at the patient’s room or bed and said, “Amitabha Buddha! The patient is dying very soon!”
chapterbody 8: The patient whom I was pointing at really passed away within “two hours.”
chapterbody 10: People asked, “Why is that?”
chapterbody 12: I answered, “All the ghost relatives of the dying patient arrive after hearing the news. Ghosts know when to come to pick up their relative.” (It is indeed really accurate!)
chapterbody 14: People asked, “Are they not being picked up by the netherworld guards?”
chapterbody 16: I answered, “Ordinary people are usually picked up by their ghost relatives. As for those picked up by netherworld guards, they have heavier karma, and are sent to hell.”
chapterbody 18: At one time, while my relative was dying, she saw her deceased husband coming to pick her up. She also saw her deceased son and a group of her deceased relatives and friends.
chapterbody 20: I said, “Not good! She will become a ghost!”
chapterbody 22: I took out my mala and hung it on my relative.
chapterbody 24: As soon as it was hung on her, all the ghosts disappeared.
chapterbody 26: I only saw the Three Saints of the Western Paradise appear in the sky, and then delivered my relative to the Western Heaven.
chapterbody 28: At the point of her death, my relative said that she could initially see the arrival of all her ghost relatives, but once my mala was hung upon her, all the ghosts disappeared and she was taken and delivered to the Western Heaven.
chapterbody 30: There was another time I saw a netherworld guard holding a hand pillory, which is used for locking up people. That netherworld guard was walking around, eventually entering an operating room. I thought the patient must be dead, and would be locked away in the middle of the operation.
chapterbody 32: Surprisingly, the one being locked up was not the patient.
chapterbody 34: It was the operation surgeon.
chapterbody 36: I could only hear a period of big confusion in the operating room.
chapterbody 38: The one who was carried out was not the patient; it was the surgeon who wore a white robe. According to what I heard, halfway through the operation, the surgeon collapsed and fainted on the ground, and unexpectedly died. They also said overwork was the cause of the surgeon’s death.
chapterbody 40: I saw very clearly that the netherworld guard went in and out of the operating room.
chapterbody 42: I also saw a nurse ghost who wore a white nurse cap and a white nurse uniform.
chapterbody 44: The strange thing was, this nurse ghost walked around in the hospital, went in and out of all the patients’ rooms, examined every patient, measured blood pressure, took their temperature, and kept patients warm with blankets, among many acts.
chapterbody 46: I noticed that the name tag of the nurse ghost was, “Wen Cuihua.”
chapterbody 48: I asked, “Is there a nurse called Wen Cuihua in the hospital?”
chapterbody 50: The medical staff answered, “Yes, she passed away a week ago because of pancreatic cancer. Although she found out she had cancer, she had never stopped working until she died. Her spirit was very admirable.”
chapterbody 53: I said, “I see her still working in the hospital!”
chapterbody 54: The medical staff said, “Who (what ghost) do you think you’re kidding?”
chapterbody 55: I was speechless because the nurse ghost Wen Cuihua was directly facing me and laughing!
chapterbody 58: Ghosts are manipulating the world’s progress in civilization, science and medicine, creative works of art, and the inventions of inventors.
chapterbody 67: The medical staff continued, “Every one of us in the hospital admires this nurse. She was highly responsible and often worked very hard without complaining.”

chapterbody: emember the television announcement?

chapterbody 11: For the third win, it was probably good luck.
chapterbody 12: For the fourth win, it really surprised people.
chapterbody 13: For the fifth win, it made people feel that this was inconceivable.

chapterbody 15: Brother Octopus was quite “accurate” in his magical predictions.
chapterbody 16: The car owner drove the car again. He was very happy because the car was really working fine!
chapterbody 17: Those losing countries really hated Brother Octopus.
chapterbody 18: One needs to believe that ghosts really do exist. This is true.
chapterbody 19: They really wanted to:
chapterbody 21: “Eat octopus sashimi!”
chapterbody 22: With so many ghosts all appearing, it meant something big was going to happen.
chapterbody 23: “Stir-fry the octopus!”
chapterbody 24: Sheng-yen Lu
chapterbody 25: “Boil the octopus for soup!”
chapterbody 27: “Slice the octopus and eat with dipping sauce!”
chapterbody 29: The losing countries wanted to eat up Brother Octopus. I had a big laugh after watching [on TV]. It was because when I was watching the television news report, I had already discovered with my Divine Eyes that there was a big ghost behind Brother Octopus.
chapterbody 31: That ghost had green eyes.
chapterbody 33: His eyeballs were continuously rolling.
chapterbody 35: His brain was quite developed with a great ability of predicting.
chapterbody 37: The prediction was so accurate because it was the ghost behind Brother Octopus manipulating him, and it had nothing to do with Brother Octopus himself!
chapterbody 39: I, Grand Master Lu, already knew and saw it. Ghosts are manipulating the world’s progress in civilization, science and medicine, creative works of art, and the inventions of inventors.
chapterbody 41: You should believe:
chapterbody 43: Ghosts exist.
chapterbody 45: Gods exist.
chapterbody 47: There was a man who bought a new car.
chapterbody 49: When he drove the car, the steering wheel would tremble vigorously by itself. He went to discuss this with the car dealer company.
chapterbody 50: I saw a ghost flee away from the steering wheel. It flew and disappeared to the south-east direction!
chapterbody 51: The car dealer agent said, “None of the cars of the same brand and same model have this kind of problem occurring. It only happens to your car.”
chapterbody 53: No trembling.
chapterbody 54: The owner drove.
chapterbody 56: It trembled again.
chapterbody 57: It was really strange!
chapterbody 59: The car dealer agent said, “It is our responsibility to fix your car until the steering wheel is completely not shaking.”
chapterbody 61: In order to fix the car, they had to find out the cause of the problem. They did not know what to do because they could not find out any reason. When the mechanic drove the car, the steering wheel did not shake.
chapterbody 62: The car was returned to the owner.
chapterbody 67: The car dealer agent test-drove the car.
chapterbody 68: When the car owner started the car, the steering wheel trembled again!

chaptername: 010 Ghost Shadows That Appeared at a Baseball Field
chapterbody: was delighted to go and watch a baseball game. When I arrived at the baseball field, it was fully packed with people. This ball game really attracted thousands of people, as all the seats were filled up. Many were left standing.
chapterbody 3: Each of the two teams had its own “fensi” [fans]. The cheerleaders demonstrated all kinds of skillful techniques, and the atmosphere was extremely lively.
chapterbody 5: Flag banners, hats, and whistles appeared one after another.
chapterbody 7: I observed for a short while, then quietly departed from the field, and dared not stay long.
chapterbody 9: Why?
chapterbody 11: It was because I saw many many “ghosts” there also watching the game. I certainly realized that with these many ghosts around, there was going to be a problem. Just like the saying: “One does not visit a temple without a cause.” With so many ghosts all appearing, it meant something big was going to happen.
chapterbody 13: I left!
chapterbody 15: I did not continue watching the ball game.
chapterbody 17: This is because I am a person who never stays long in “a place of trouble” and never goes to “a place of trouble.”
chapterbody 19: Later!
chapterbody 21: When watched from the television, I saw that when the ball game was half way through, the players started to argue with one another due to the problem of the unfair referee. Both teams ended up fighting. The “fans” who were supporting their own teams went down to the field to join the fighting, creating a situation of two armies with clubs flailing. The situation got out of control, with many wounded in action. Some of them had fractured bones, and some were even trampled to death. It was chaotic.
chapterbody 23: People can, in an instant, lose all rationality.
chapterbody 25: Emotions boil over.
chapterbody 27: How could they be so impulsive?
chapterbody 29: My answer to this is, “When there are too many ghosts around, people will naturally lose control.”
chapterbody 31: There was another occasion:
chapterbody 33: Again, it was a ball game played by celebrities. There was also a multitude of people who arrived and filled up the entire observation platform.
chapterbody 35: This time, it should be a peaceful match because not too many “ghosts” were attending.
chapterbody 37: I felt so much relieved.
chapterbody 39: I only saw one “obstacle ghost” arrive.
chapterbody 41: This “obstacle ghost” seemed to know me.
chapterbody 43: He asked, “Grand Master Lu watches the ball game?”
chapterbody 45: I answered, “I am a baseball fan.”
chapterbody 47: He asked, “You don’t have another purpose for coming to watch the ball game, do you?”
chapterbody 49: I answered, “What other purpose? I simply came to watch the game.”
chapterbody 51: He questioned, “You will not use your power to interfere with me?”
chapterbody 52: I replied, “We do not owe each other anything, why should I interfere with you?”
chapterbody 55: In the field, I saw the “obstacle ghost” suddenly emerge from the east, from the west, from the south, and from the north. The “obstacle ghost” was running here and there; who knows what he was doing?
chapterbody 57: Eventually he was carried out of the field. It is said he could not participate in any match for a year.
chapterbody 59: The obstacle ghost was very happy.
chapterbody 61: I asked, “Why did you do that?”
chapterbody 62: The obstacle ghost replied, “That is what he (the national champion) owes me.”
chapterbody 63: The obstacle ghost said, “I feel relieved then!”
chapterbody 64: At this time, as a national champion was running between bases, I saw the obstacle ghost use his body to block him. No sooner said than done, this national champion leaned forward and crushed the leg that was sliding towards the base. With a cracking sound, his leg bone broke and the tendon got twisted. He fell to the ground and was unable to get up.
chapterbody 69: Even doctors and scientists were completely baffled and did not know why a healthy person would just suddenly die.

chaptername: 011 Vampire Ghosts Spoken by Master Lian-zhi
chapterbody: here was once a dinner gathering.
chapterbody 2: After one week, the coffin was re-opened by everyone. Amazing! Amazing! The dead bodies had already started to decompose. The clothes that were originally new and bright, were now already bitten and damaged by the insects. They really became dead bodies.
chapterbody 3: Master Lian-zhi mentioned a true story about “Vampire Ghosts”:
chapterbody 5: There was a fellow disciple, who had a big family.
chapterbody 6: He answered, “Grand Master Lu lives so far away in Seattle, United States, and is very busy. It was when there was really no one else to consult, that I then started to remember.”
chapterbody 7: A family member passed away, but one month after the completion of all the funeral services, another person passed away.
chapterbody 9: They thought that it was just coincidence and once again finished all the funeral services. But after twenty days later, a third person passed away.
chapterbody 11: The strange thing was, this third family member was a young person who should not have died. A sense of foreboding permeated in the family.
chapterbody 13: They hired a reverend to conduct purification and blessing ceremonies, and thought that everything should be fine. Unexpectedly, a fourth person died thereafter.
chapterbody 15: Everyone in the family was panicking. Four family members had consecutively died in less than half a year.
chapterbody 17: It was even more peculiar that a fifth person soon died!
chapterbody 19: They hired another well-experienced Voodoo master to perform the “Body Substitution Practice,” and thought that with the help of a “substitute body” they could have peace in their mind.
chapterbody 21: A sixth person died yet again!
chapterbody 23: Oh my heavens! In less than a year, six family members died. Feng shui masters were hired to examine the “residence of the living” and the “burial grounds of the deceased.” The feng shui masters took a brief look and made some feng shui alterations.
chapterbody 25: Once again, a seventh person died!
chapterbody 27: The family was about to go insane and crazy. Everyone was so worried that he or she will be the next in line.
chapterbody 29: Again, they hired eminent monks to conduct the “Emperor Liang Repentance,” the “Great Compassion Repentance,” the “Water Repentance,” the “Lotus Sutra Repentance,” the “Yogacara Ulkamukha (The Ritual for Feeding of Hungry Ghosts)” the “Offering of Food to the Spirits,” etc...
chapterbody 30: An eighth person died yet again!
chapterbody 32: Help! Help! [They would hire] any popular reverend, Voodoo master, feng shui master, naturalist, taoist, astrologer, supreme master, master, living buddha, exorcist, etc …..
chapterbody 34: Even doctors and scientists were completely baffled and did not know why a healthy person would just suddenly die.
chapterbody 36: A ninth person died yet again!
chapterbody 38: Oh Heavens! Oh Heavens! What else could be done?
chapterbody 40: At this time, that fellow disciple who had taken refuge in Grand Master Lu happened to remember Grand Master Lu who was living far away in Seattle, United States.
chapterbody 42: Nine family members died in a year.
chapterbody 44: Is it not scary? They were making funeral arrangements almost daily.
chapterbody 45: Once I did the divination.
chapterbody 47: The “Heavenly Official” and “Earth Killing” (Zi Wei Dou Shu) stars firmly stated that: “The paternal great-grandfather and great-grandmother were buried in a vampire land and have been transformed into vampire ghosts. Every night they fly out and absorb energy from living people, who in turn die when their energy is completely exhausted. If this continues on, more people will die.”
chapterbody 49: I told Master Lian-zhi to go quickly and help.
chapterbody 50: I asked, “Why did you not consult me at the beginning?”
chapterbody 51: Choose a “Remove Day” afternoon to open the coffin.
chapterbody 52: Grab a handful of muddy soil and throw onto the bodies of the vampire ghosts.
chapterbody 55: Master Lian-zhi followed my instructions and together with a group of brave family members, went up to the grave site. When the coffin was opened, everyone was almost scared to death at the first glance!
chapterbody 58: Hairs had grown longer. (horrible)
chapterbody 63: This was the method to save [the living].
chapterbody 64: Both great-grandfather and great-grandmother were wearing burial clothes, hats, and shoes, and they were lying there as if alive.
chapterbody 65: Their faces looked alive with two fangs exposed. Exactly “vampire ghosts.”
chapterbody 66: When everyone saw the bodies, several of them stumbled off and ran away. The remaining people followed my edict to throw the muddy soil into the coffin and closed it.

chaptername: 012 What was Being Carried in the Motorcycle’s Backseat?
chapterbody: ne year, I returned to Taichung, Taiwan.
chapterbody 3: On a street of Taichung, I saw a True Buddha School disciple riding a motorcycle and quickly passing by in front of me.
chapterbody 5: I saw him, but he did not notice me. I saw on his back seat, a sexy girl with long hair. Dressed in a tiny outfit with legs exposed, the girl had both arms around the disciple.
chapterbody 7: I was thinking in my mind:
chapterbody 9: “Great! I could tease him tomorrow.” (He was married, had a wife, and still carried a sexy girl everywhere.)
chapterbody 11: The next day.
chapterbody 13: I asked, “Yesterday, around two o’clock in the afternoon, did you ride a motorcycle and pass by Wuquan Road?”
chapterbody 15: He answered frankly, “Grand Master, you saw me?”
chapterbody 17: I said, “Yes, who was riding in the backseat? What is your relationship with her?”
chapterbody 19: He was horrified.
chapterbody 21: I thought I had caught a “criminal.”
chapterbody 23: He said, “I carried no one in the backseat!”
chapterbody 25: I questioned, “She has long hair, wears a tight mini-skirt with exposed fair legs. I saw very clearly. Her two arms were tightly entwining your waist, and you deliberately pretended not to see me.”
chapterbody 27: He was greatly startled, “Grand Master, I really did not carry anyone. Could Grand Master be mistaken? Please do not falsely accuse your disciple!”
chapterbody 29: “You do not lie to Grand Master,” I said.
chapterbody 31: “I dare not,” he said.
chapterbody 33: At this time, I felt rather tense. This disciple never lies to people. If he said he did not carry anyone, he really meant it. But, what did I see? Could it be the “life-claiming ghost?”
chapterbody 35: I used “Divination through the Fingers.” It was really a “life-claiming ghost” sitting in the backseat, I saw it using my Divine Eyes.
chapterbody 37: I had to help my disciple, I said:
chapterbody 39: Use a piece of red cloth and write down your personal eight characters of birth time, name, and address; then cut nails and hairs, wrap them all together and tie with red string.
chapterbody 41: Pick an afternoon of a “Remove Day.”
chapterbody 43: Hang the pouch in front of Avalokitesvara Bodhisattva’s chest.
chapterbody 45: Pray for blessing and protection. (Then, nothing bad would happen.)
chapterbody 47: A year passed by as usual and everything was fine.
chapterbody 49: Later: On the twenty-fourth day of the first month of the new lunar year, while he was riding the motorcycle, he fell off from the motorcycle without reason. There was no accident too. He was quickly delivered to the hospital, but he died.
chapterbody 51: The doctor diagnosed that he died suddenly due to a cerebral hemorrhage (stroke).
chapterbody 52: When I heard that he had passed away, I could not believe it since he was still young.
chapterbody 55: I went to his house.
chapterbody 57: I asked his wife, “Where is the red cloth pouch?”
chapterbody 59: His wife answered, “There was a red cloth pouch, but during the year-end cleanup, I saw the red cloth pouch was covered with dust. I took it out and threw it away!”
chapterbody 61: “Oh Heavens!” I cried.
chapterbody 62: His wife questioned, “Is his death related to the red cloth pouch?”
chapterbody 63: Even though there was a “life-claiming ghost,” but I had already performed a special technique and he had the protection from Avalokitesvara Bodhisattva. How could he die?
chapterbody 64: I found that the shrine in his house had no red cloth pouch hanging on the chest of the principal deity, Avalokitesvara Bodhisattva.
chapterbody 69: I explained to his wife the entire matter in detail. Now that he was taken away by the “life-claiming ghost,” I must use my power to get him back and deliver him to the pure land of Maha Twin Lotus Ponds!
chapterbody 71: Yin and Yang are different, human and ghosts are unlike. You consume energy, but humans consume tangible food.

chapterbody: he father of my daughter-in-law Shanshan Lu had passed away. My son, Fo-chi Lu, and Shanshan Lu brought back from Indonesia some of his ashes, which were placed in the lower level of the shrine, for memorial purpose.
chapterbody 3: Unexpectedly, the soul of the father-in-law returned with them and stayed in Fo-chi Lu’s home in Seattle.
chapterbody 5: Shanshan Lu could sense it.
chapterbody 7: Fo-chi Lu could sense it.
chapterbody 9: For example:

chapterbody 28: Both hands manifested a ray of white light that radiated out and forced his father-in-law to retreat. Instantly, he disappeared.
chapterbody 30: And more:

chapterbody 34: The young couple told me about it.
chapterbody 36: I went to their home and performed water purification. Wherever I sprinkled the purified water, he quickly shunned away. I completely purified the whole house.
chapterbody 38: He was forced to move outside the house.
chapterbody 40: He said, “Master Lu (in-law), I cannot live outside. If I ever get seen by the Day Patrol God and the Night Patrol God, I will be taken away.”
chapterbody 41: I said, “Souls return to souls, dust returns to dust. You go to find your own destiny!”
chapterbody 43: He said, “I wish to look after my daughter Shanshan Lu.”
chapterbody 45: I said, “I understand that you want to protect your daughter, but their ordinary daily life will be affected if it is too obvious. It would be better if I deliver you to the Buddha pure land!”
chapterbody 47: He said, “I am not familiar and I am afraid. I do not want to go.”
chapterbody 48: (He was a Catholic when he was alive, but he was not a staunch practitioner. He could neither go to heaven nor hell.)
chapterbody 50: I asked, “Where do you want to go?”
chapterbody 53: I said, “I will transform the exquisite seven-layer pagoda into a house for you, and you can live in there! Remember, remain invisible as you are invisible. You cannot manifest to anyone anymore. Yin and Yang are different, human and ghosts are unlike. You consume energy, but humans consume tangible food. They provide offerings to you and you bless them. You also cannot affect my grandson Hong Lu and granddaughter Gin Lu. Would that be fine?”
chapterbody 54: He answered, “Fine!”
chapterbody 56: From then on, the soul of the father-in-law never appeared again!
chapterbody 59: The god of rain and god of wind all listened to my commands.
chapterbody 61: Sheng-yen Lu
chapterbody 67: He replied, “Stay beside Shanshan!”

chaptername: 014 The Great Ceremony Held on the Beach
chapterbody: conducted the “Wildlife Release Ritual” on November 13, 2011 at half past one in the afternoon on the beach of Wushi Harbor of Toucheng Township, Yilan County.
chapterbody 2: In front of his bed, I opened my father’s heavenly gate which was directly connected to the Buddha pure land.
chapterbody 3: This extra-large outdoor ceremony on the “beach” was organized by:
chapterbody 5: Master Lianyue.
chapterbody 6: Master Lianmiao.
chapterbody 7: Master Liandeng.
chapterbody 8: Dharma sister Zou Yilan.
chapterbody 9: Toucheng Township Mayor. (Chen Xiuyuan).
chapterbody 11: This was also the first time in my life conducting a large ceremony on a beach facing the sea and “Turtle Hill Island”; it was really unprecedented.
chapterbody 13: Its characteristics were:
chapterbody 15: An extra high “dharma platform” was built on the beach, with its first-class sound and lighting features. (Also included were the Taiko Divine Drum and Nine Heavens Divine Drum.)
chapterbody 17: On the dragon side of the beach, a “Kalachakra” sand mandala was set up with extraordinary grandeur.
chapterbody 19: In front of Kalachakra, a statue of “Golden Mother of the Jade
chapterbody 20: Pond” was vividly crafted using sand mandala.
chapterbody 22: There were altogether thirty-three stacks of little sand mandalas in the surrounding symbolizing Indra’s Heaven, with thirty-three gods.
chapterbody 24: Fish fry were directly released into the sea in relay style. These kinds of fish fry belong to the same kind of fish which are richly produced in Wushi Harbor. Thus, the “same kind” entered into the “same kind.”
chapterbody 26: The miracle was:
chapterbody 28: Wushi Harbor had heavy rainfall continuously for over half a month, there was not one day without heavy rainfall, the rain stopped only on the day of the ceremony. After the ceremony finished, the rain continued once again non-stop.
chapterbody 30: Wushi Harbor had very strong gusts of wind everyday with wind speeds of around level eight to ten. When I was seated at my dharma throne, I called, “Wind stop.”
chapterbody 32: The gusty wind stopped at once.
chapterbody 34: I (Living Buddha Lian-sheng), really can call upon the wind and rain.
chapterbody 35: I called the rain to stop, the rain really stopped.
chapterbody 36: I called the wind to stop, the wind really ceased.

chapterbody: was born at the Niuchou riverbank of “Back Lake” of Chiayi. In the year of 1945, the American army aircrafts bombed Chiayi. My parents fled and stayed at the chicken farm at Niuchou River and I was born at the chicken house.

chaptername: 017 Thirty-six Small Statues of Ksitigarbha Bodhisattva
chapterbody: sitigarbha Bodhisattva and I have a profound affinity. He frequently appears in my writings. He is my big brother and I address him as “Big Brother Ksitigarbha.”
chapterbody 3: Sometimes we are like close friends and can talk about anything. We are like glue and paint, there is no distinction between the two of us.
chapterbody 5: Sometimes we are like father and son.
chapterbody 7: He is one of my three principal deities, “The Great Vow Ksitigarbha Bodhisattva.”
chapterbody 9: Ksitigarbha Bodhisattva is very good to me. Good to an extreme degree. Moreover, I can go to Ksitigarbha Bodhisattva’s pure land “Cui Wei Pure Land.” It seems like I am the only one who can go there.
chapterbody 11: Besides, Ksitigarbha Bodhisattva commanded one of his ten Great Ghost Kings, the “Smiling Face Ghost King,” to accompany me and protect me while I preach in the mundane world. Some of the vicious ghosts and demons, once they see the appearance of the “Smiling Face Ghost King,” will immediately escape. The “Smiling Face Ghost King” is hidden in my right thumb. My thumbprint bears the appearance of the “Smiling Face Ghost King.”
chapterbody 13: One evening, there were thirty-six small statues of Ksitigarbha Bodhisattva suddenly appeared. These thirty-six statues wore the same facial expression and the Five Buddha Crown; each statue held a monk’s staff in one hand and a precious pearl in the other hand. Each of them sat on “Diting, the Unicorn Beast.”
chapterbody 15: They told me, “Master Lu, you must release us, you must, you must.”
chapterbody 17: I was astonished, “What is going on?”
chapterbody 19: The thirty-six Ksitigarbha statues said together, “You will know tomorrow, be sure to release us.”
chapterbody 21: The next day, I was invited by a Buddhist temple to examine the geography of the temple. The scale of this Buddhist temple was not small, and could be considered as magnificent.
chapterbody 23: The abbot said, “This temple was quite small originally. Later on, since the response to prayers was efficacious, the local people raised money to build a big temple. I then became the abbot. However, contrary to expectations, after the big temple was built, fewer people came to offer incense. With fewer pilgrims coming, the front courtyard became desolate.”
chapterbody 25: I went into every hall to pay homage.
chapterbody 27: When I arrived at the Ksitigarbha hall pagoda, I saw a huge gold-plated standing Ksitigarbha Bodhisattva statue.
chapterbody 29: I asked, “The temple is well established, why is it not prosperous?”
chapterbody 31: Ksitigarbha Bodhisattva replied, “Just waiting for Master Lu to arrive, then it will be prosperous.”
chapterbody 33: I asked, “Why is that?”
chapterbody 35: Ksitigarbha Bodhisattva answered, “In the past, the temple was small but very prosperous; today, the temple is big but declining. It is because people do not understand the reason behind growth and decline and do not trace back to how it originated. The original thirty-six small Ksitigarbha Bodhisattva statues were abandoned in the storage room with nobody worshipping, and that is the reason for the decline.”
chapterbody 37: I questioned, “Are the thirty-six small Ksitigarbha Bodhisattva statues that important?”
chapterbody 39: He answered, “The temple was small, but full of spiritual responses because of the spiritual response power of the small Ksitigarbhas; once the big temple was built, those thirty-six small statues of Ksitigarbha were all abandoned in the storage room, how could the temple avoid declining?”
chapterbody 41: I asked, “Are not the current Buddhas and Bodhisattvas much bigger and statelier?”
chapterbody 43: He answered, “The size of the Bodhisattvas does not correlate to the strength of divine efficacy.”
chapterbody 45: I asked the abbot, “You locked up all the thirty-six small statues of Ksitigarbha Bodhisattva which hailed from the founding days of the [small] temple, is that right?”
chapterbody 47: The abbot replied, “I do not know. My predecessor relocated the small statues of the Bodhisattva since he felt we already have the big statues. I do not know where they are now located.”
chapterbody 49: I said, “Storage room.”
chapterbody 51: The abbot led me to the storage room. Once opened, a moldy smell struck my nose. Spider webs and dust were everywhere, and junk was all piled up. The small Ksitigarbhas were found among the junk. After counting, it was exactly thirty-six statues.
chapterbody 52: The abbot exclaimed in astonishment, “Master Lu is really amazing!”

chapterbody 7: She was a fair and delicate-looking girl. Her face was suddenly changed and she looked completely like a different person.
chapterbody 9: The face had a sinister twist.
chapterbody 10: The eyes had a savage glint.
chapterbody 11: The tongue stuck out from the open mouth.
chapterbody 12: The dense white teeth wanted to bite people.
chapterbody 14: Everyone felt fear when they saw this, and dodged her.

chapterbody 18: A “boom” sound rang and the little girl who was originally baring her teeth and brandishing her claws and running towards me, suddenly fell on the ground and vomited white foam. After a while, she woke up and was back to her usual fair and delicate-looking self.
chapterbody 20: Everyone applauded.
chapterbody 22: However, after a short while, the “Strange Spirit” once again “possessed” the girl, and her face changed to that of a flesh-biting ghost. She pounced towards me.
chapterbody 24: This time round, I was not going to be courteous, and the “Smiling Face Ghost King” appeared. Both my hands formed the “Flying Wheel Mudra” and circled it above the head of the little girl who continuously cried, “I dare not do it anymore! I dare not do it anymore!”
chapterbody 26: “Plonk.” She fell on the ground again.
chapterbody 28: The “Strange Spirit” really ran away.
chapterbody 30: This time, the little girl recovered to become a normal person, and the strange spirit no longer possessed her body anymore.
chapterbody 32: The mother who brought the little girl repeatedly asked me, “What to do? What to do? When she returns to California, she will be possessed by the strange spirit again. What are we going to do?”
chapterbody 34: I contemplated seriously…
chapterbody 36: I heard a voice saying, “Grand Master Lu! I can take care of this matter, everything will naturally be resolved.”

chapterbody 40: On the shrine, there was a statue who wore a general’s crown and an armor; his right hand held a double-edged sword and his feet rested on a tortoise and a snake. This holy statue was radiating with fine lights. It was him who opened his mouth talking.
chapterbody 42: He was:
chapterbody 44: “Xuan Tian Shang Di, the Devil-Sweeping Celestial Venerable of the North Pole.”
chapterbody 46: I took a look, and then said, “Excellent! Excellent! The little girl will be saved!”
chapterbody 48: I drew a “Shang Yan” talisman and blessed the little girl again.
chapterbody 50: It was reported that after returning to California, the little girl’s “possessions” became fewer and fewer, and eventually she became completely normal.
chapterbody 52: The little girl pointed to Xuan Tian Shang Di, the Devil-Sweeping Celestial Venerable of the North Pole and said, “It was this Venerable who saved me.”
chapterbody 55: It was because Xuan Tian Shang Di, the Devil-Sweeping Celestial Venerable of the North Pole, wanted to protect the little girl, I did not even inform her mother nor anybody else.
chapterbody 58: The little girl specifically pointed out that that the God who saved her was exactly “Xuan Tian Shang Di, the Devil Sweeping Celestial Venerable of the North Pole.”

Great reverends, living buddhas, masters, Taoist masters, spirit mediums, etc… All said, “It is incurable.” Later on, when they heard that I was in Seattle and wanted to find me, the Great Reverend said, “It is also useless to find Sheng-Yen Lu, since my power is stronger than Sheng-Yen Lu’s.” Well, the result is for all to witness!)
chapterbody 62: I, Grand Master Lu, actually dare not boast the strength of my own powers. I only go with the natural flow of things to save sentient beings. “The decree is with me, all gods will come to help.”

chapterbody: here was a Hong Kong entrepreneur, dharma brother Zheng, who researched on his own and invented many exquisite Vajrayana dharma instruments.

chapterbody 12: It was needless to say that they were exquisitely made and they had an elegant quality. Every piece of work included the dignified totems such as parasols, eight auspicious symbols, and five-colored clouds. The Vajrayana dharma instruments invented by dharma brother Zheng were modular, the pieces could be disassembled and regrouped again, just like the “Transformers.” We were so amazed after seeing them.
chapterbody 14: Dharma brother Zheng’s dharma instruments were free gifts. He was very supportive of the “Sheng-yen Lu Foundation,” so as long as you made a donation to the “Foundation” for a certain amount, you would receive dharma brother Zheng’s dharma instruments as free gifts. These were very precious free gifts, as the cost of making them was not cheap. These dharma instruments had nothing to do with Brother Zheng’s business. He wholeheartedly supported “True Buddha School” using his money and energy. Everything was given free, so he gave himself a moniker, “Bam Bam” which means Dumb, dumb.
chapterbody 16: I laughed at him once, “At the rate you are doing this, would your company not close down eventually?”
chapterbody 18: He answered, “I am just goofing around.”
chapterbody 20: Strangely enough, after I uttered my joke, “Would your company not close down,” dharma brother Zheng’s company, no longer received a single order. Not even one order. Without any order, what could the company’s production line produce?
chapterbody 22: Dharma brother Zheng became very anxious and he found me, “After Grand Master’s one phrase, ‘Would your company not close down,’ I do not have orders coming in. It is exactly like what you said. Grand Master, I pray to you to help and just say the golden words at your dharma throne: ‘to let orders come to dharma brother Zheng’s company!’”
chapterbody 24: I asked, “Will it be like this?”
chapterbody 26: He replied, “Grand Master is a Buddha, Buddhas do not say false words.”
chapterbody 28: That day, while I was giving a dharma talk at my dharma throne in Taiwan Lei Tsang Temple, I said, “Let dharma brother Zheng’s company receive orders! Let his business thrive and be prosperous!”
chapterbody 30: Strangely enough, once he returned to Hong Kong, he immediately received a big order. Currently, his business has been picking up and becoming more and more prosperous. It is really worthy of celebration. Are my words efficacious? It is really strange.
chapterbody 32: I said, “Closing down!” The company received no order.
chapterbody 33: I said, “Thriving!” The company’s orders then arrived.
chapterbody 34: This is quite strange, does my mouth have such a strong spiritual efficacy?
chapterbody 36: And:
chapterbody 38: Three weeks before I conducted a ceremony on the beach at Wushi Harbor of Toucheng Township of Yilan County, there were heavy rainfalls every day. On the day I conducted the dharma ceremony, the rain stopped. It was the only day that the rain stopped.
chapterbody 40: I was on my dharma throne and also said, “Once the ceremony is completed, you can continue the heavy rainfall!”
chapterbody 42: I should not have said this sentence, because heavy rainfall resumed that evening.
chapterbody 44: This time, with continuous heavy rainfall in Hualian, Taitung, and Yilan counties, lots of crops were completely destroyed. There were also falling stones, mudslides, and floods. Roads were closed, streams surged, cars were covered by water to the roof, and lots of residential houses had water flooding in.
chapterbody 46: I think, I cannot arbitrarily make jokes anymore, since they become true once I open my mouth.
chapterbody 48: Buddhas do not say false words!

chapterbody: n November 2011, Thailand suffered from a major flood. Its loss was really immeasurable.
chapterbody 3: Three causes of the major flood were reported:
chapterbody 5: 1. Abnormal weather.
chapterbody 6: 2. Geographical factor.
chapterbody 7: 3. Human negligence.
chapterbody 9: Abnormal weather generated the heavy rainfalls. The geographical factor was the estuary to the Pacific Ocean usually flows smoothly, but due to a period of [geographical] anomaly, the water inundated over the embankment. Human negligence was the poor water drainage facilities throughout the whole of Thailand and storm water drainage engineering to cope with flood.
chapterbody 11: Thailand was flooded, even the capital Bangkok could not avoid it. Instantly, Bangkok was thirty percent flooded, fearing that even the seventy percent would be soon flooded too.
chapterbody 13: True Buddha School has a chapter “Mi Xing Tang” in Thailand, located in Bangkok. There was a disciple who prayed to “Padmakumara.”
chapterbody 15: That night. Grand Master Lu appeared in the dream of the disciple and said to the disciple, “You follow me!”
chapterbody 17: Grand Master brought the disciple to a place and said, “You take a look!”
chapterbody 19: That place then revealed a big crack. All the flooding water formed into a big maelstrom and was gradually swallowed up by the big mouth of the earth crack.
chapterbody 21: On November 20, 2011, the flood in Bangkok suddenly withdrew. The big flood had withdrawn, mysteriously withdrawn!
chapterbody 23: Originally, the city of Bangkok was supposed to be completely flooded. In the end, it was only thirty percent flooded, the rest of the seventy percent was completely normal.
chapterbody 25: The True Buddha disciples of “Mi Xing Tang” eulogized this matter of the earth cracking and swallowing the flood water.
chapterbody 27: In Thailand, I have my books published in Thai. The disciple who published my books in Thai is dharma sister Zhang Yufang. Her house was not flooded.
chapterbody 29: Excellent! Excellent!
chapterbody 31: The flood in Bangkok only covered thirty percent and then withdrew, what was going on? Was it because the disciple prayed to “Padmakumara”?
chapterbody 33: According to my understanding:
chapterbody 35: It was “Padmakumara” who invocated “Water Deva.” Water Deva, whose Sanskrit name is “Varuna,” is one of the Twelve Devas in Vajrayana, one of the Guardians of the Eight Directions, protecting the Heaven in the western direction, and is the king of the dragon family.
chapterbody 37: “Water Deva” is a big God in Hinduism, is the master of all the rivers, and dominates all kinds of water for human beings.
chapterbody 39: Grand Master Lu supplicated the mouth of “Water Deva” to swallow Thailand’s flood water.
chapterbody 41: I formed the “Water Deva Mudra”:
chapterbody 43: I made a fist with the left hand, the thumb is not attached within the palm, index finger upright and slightly bent.
chapterbody 45: Chanted the mantra to invocate “Water Deva”:
chapterbody 47: “Namo, san-man-duo, mu-tuo-nan, ah-ban-bo-duo-ye, so-ha.”
chapterbody 48: And: “Om, po-luo-na-ye, so-ha.”
chapterbody 49: And: “Om, mei-tuo, shi-li, so-ha.”
chapterbody 51: Therefore, “Water Deva” opened the big mouth and swallowed up all the big flood water.
chapterbody 52: Whoever has visited my private shrine in South Hill Residence, Bellevue, Seattle of the United States, already knows that I worship “Water Deva” in my private shrine.
chapterbody 55: He lives in the middle of the ocean and stands on top of a conch.
chapterbody 56: One head, two arms.
chapterbody 57: The appearance is as beautiful as a fairy, with the top half of the body naked.
chapterbody 58: Fine and exquisite body curvature, half covered with heavenly cloth and layers of skirt.
chapterbody 59: All around, there are dragons, water species, and fish surrounding, with palms put together towards “Water Deva.”
chapterbody 61: I, Grand Master Lu, must recite the holy name of “Water Deva” and chant the mantra of “Water Deva” in my daily cultivation.
chapterbody 62: Therefore, the mouth of “Water Deva” swallowed the flood water and saved Thailand from the disaster of flood.
chapterbody 63: “Water Deva” is the most beautiful deva amongst the Guardians of the Eight Directions, the Twelve Devas, and the Twenty Devas.
chapterbody 64: Wearing a “hair-bundled grass crown.”
chapterbody 69: The two Gods of Wealth and Merit had disappeared, and changed to a group of fierce and evil spirits.

chapterbody: nce I went to a restaurant for dinner. When entering the door, I was so shocked to see that there were two Gods of Wealth and Merit (Earth Gods) standing inside the door.
chapterbody 3: I smiled and greeted the two Gods of Wealth and Merit.
chapterbody 5: “Hi! How are you?”
chapterbody 7: This time, it was the two Wealth Gods’ turn to be surprised, for here was one human being who could actually see them.
chapterbody 9: The two Gods of Wealth and Merit graciously returned the greeting, “Guest, come and sit.”
chapterbody 11: I heard the two Wealth Gods whispering in the restaurant,
chapterbody 12: “Who is that?”
chapterbody 14: “Master Lu, do you not know?”
chapterbody 16: “Who is this incredible being?”
chapterbody 18: “He was Padmakumara in his past life, and he delivers sentient beings this life.”
chapterbody 20: “Who is Padmakumara then?”
chapterbody 22: “The transformation of Amitabha Buddha.”
chapterbody 24: “Ha! His background is quite impressive, no wonder he could see us.”
chapterbody 26: “He has numerous miraculous experiences.”
chapterbody 28: When I listened until here, I did not want to listen anymore. I shut off my “Clairaudience.”
chapterbody 30: I said to my disciple at the same table, “This restaurant will be greatly prosperous in the future!”
chapterbody 32: Later on, it really happened as I predicted, from one restaurant they expanded to two, and then three, four, and five restaurants…Business was extremely good and booming, with strong financial resources. The money was really rolling in continuously.
chapterbody 34: Why could I predict the restaurant becoming prosperous?
chapterbody 36: Because: The restaurant had two Gods of Wealth and Merit standing guard, how could it not boom?
chapterbody 38: Many years had gone by. I went to this restaurant again. Once I entered the door, I did not see the two Gods of Wealth and Merit; but instead, I saw several “non-human” ferocious evil ghosts running back and forth in the restaurant. I took a deep breath. And discovered that all were “evil energy.”
chapterbody 40: Actually, I was very surprised. What was going on? The two Gods of Wealth and Merit had disappeared, and changed to a group of fierce and evil spirits.
chapterbody 42: I said to the disciple at the same table, “It is over! This restaurant is doomed to fail!”
chapterbody 44: It became true again later on! Food in the restaurant ran into problems, hundreds of people had mass poisoning and needed to be hospitalized. The restaurant’s reputation had deteriorated a great deal.
chapterbody 46: In the past, it was so busy. Now, it is so quiet. The big restaurant had an external renovation, the scaffolding suddenly collapsed, a number of people were killed or wounded.
chapterbody 48: Another two restaurants caught fire with no cause or reason. The fire was quite strong, people were burnt to death. The restaurants were forced to close down business.
chapterbody 50: The owner of the restaurant went to Las Vegas and lost lots of money!
chapterbody 51: The owner had misconducts. (The owner sexually assaulted somebody’s wife and daughter.)
chapterbody 52: The shareholders became disharmonious.
chapterbody 53: Court cases one after another.
chapterbody 54: Overall, unfortunate matters happened without break. After many years’ prosperity, the business was back to square one within just a few years.
chapterbody 56: One day, I met the two Gods of Wealth and Merit. I mentioned this matter and the two gods told me what happened at the peak of the owner’s business.
chapterbody 57: The company’s driver was a kept man of the owner’s wife. A misconduct had taken place.
chapterbody 58: The owner’s misconduct was that of having three mistresses.
chapterbody 59: The restaurant’s misconduct was that of knowingly serving customers expired food.
chapterbody 60: They also participated in a debt collection organization, and fell into bad company.
chapterbody 61: Days and nights were spent gambling.
chapterbody 65: They robbed and seized shareholders’ money.
chapterbody 66: The two Gods of Wealth and Merit could not bear to see it and then left!
chapterbody 67: The owner of the restaurant played with stocks and got stuck in the stock market.
