chaptername: 022 Bought Two Small Plots of Land
chapterbody: hen I was living in the United States of America, I bought two small plots of land. It was a sales agent who took me to see the lands and I bought them.
chapterbody 3: The first plot of land was far away from my residency. From Highway No. 5, go north, then from Exit 212, turn west and arrive at a peninsula which I called “Ke Mi Te” (Camano Island). The driving time is about one and a half hours.
chapterbody 5: This land was facing the Pacific Ocean. It was where I met the “Eight Great Dragon Kings.” Back then, that area was quite desolate and sparsely populated, and therefore the price was cheap. I bought it with the aim of building the “True Buddha University.” (That was what I had in mind.)
chapterbody 6: The land occupied 130 acres.
chapterbody 8: I recited the Local Earth Deity mantra:
chapterbody 10: “Namo san-man-duo, mu-tuo-nan, om, du-lu-du-lu, di-wei, suo-ha.”
chapterbody 12: I stamped my left foot on the ground three times. A Local Earth Deity came out.
chapterbody 14: I asked, “It is alright to buy this land?”
chapterbody 16: He replied, “Yes.”
chapterbody 18: I asked, “Any profit?”
chapterbody 19: It
chapterbody 20: He answered, “Steady growth.”
chapterbody 22: Therefore, I bought the land. It was really “steady growth,” the price had gone up, but comparatively slow.
chapterbody 24: And:
chapterbody 26: Another plot of land was located at the south of my residency. From Highway 405, go south, and arrive at “Kent” area. The land occupied only thirty acres.
chapterbody 28: Back then, the surroundings of that land were equally desolate and uninhabited; it was a big piece of forest land with overgrown weeds everywhere. There was only one supermarket in the neighborhood, and not even one residential housing.That plot of land was also very cheap.
chapterbody 30: I recited the Local Earth Deity mantra:
chapterbody 32: “Namo san-man-duo, mu-tuo-nan, om, du-lu-du-lu, di-wei, suo-ha.”
chapterbody 34: I stamped my left foot on the ground three times. Again a Local Earth Deity came out.
chapterbody 36: I asked, “It is alright to buy this land?”
chapterbody 37: He replied, “Yes.”
chapterbody 39: I asked, “Any profit?”
chapterbody 41: He did not reply, he only used his hand to draw a circle in the space, and I was very surprised once I saw it. Heavens! From the circle revealed numerous commercial avenues and shopping malls which had densely populated residential communities in the neighborhood with busy traffic. It was a very bustling scenery.
chapterbody 43: I was shocked.
chapterbody 45: I asked, “How much it will gain?”
chapterbody 47: He answered, “Twenty times.”
chapterbody 49: I bought it without a second thought.
chapterbody 51: Subsequently, it was really like that. Originally, it was a piece of desolate and uninhabited land. The government rezoning the land for commercial district with residential communities behind it. Within a few years, changed from nothing to be a new city, from a deserted land to be a prosperous area. The price of my plot of land had gone up twenty times. My God! It was really scary.
chapterbody 52: And:
chapterbody 53: Years ago, I passed by a plot of land. I saw that this plot of land was actually sparkling and radiating so much brilliance that I was unable to open my eyes.
chapterbody 54: I said, “This land belongs to the world’s richest man!” (I did not have money to buy at that time.)
chapterbody 56: It became true later on. That plot of land was bought by Bill Gates for building the Microsoft Corporate. Bill Gates became the richest man in the world. Once again, “the divination is really accurate.”

chaptername: 023 White Lotuses Emanate from My Mouth
chapterbody: ne day, as I passed by a large monastery, the sound of sutra recitation wafted from it, and so with curiosity, I went in.
chapterbody 3: The monastery was actually holding a ceremony. In the center, there was one person who had a shaved head and he wore a yellow cassock with a red precept robe on the outside.
chapterbody 5: Surrounding him were two rows of female lay Buddhists wearing black Haiqing robes and each of them was holding a sutra for recitation.
chapterbody 7: Behind the black Haiqing robes, there was a group of believers, not too many of them; but, they filled up the whole main hall.
chapterbody 9: I listened carefully to the scriptures of the sutra, it was the “Avalokitesvara Bodhisattva Pu Men Pin,” a chapter within the [The Saddharmapundarīka Sutra] (The Lotus Sutra).
chapterbody 11: The believers who were reciting and praying were very pious. Their heads were lowered; only the sound of sutra reciting was heard. There was no other sound.
chapterbody 13: There were three golden statues in the main hall and they were:
chapterbody 15: Fundamental Teacher Shakyamuni Buddha in the middle.
chapterbody 16: Medicine Vaidurya Light King Buddha on the right side.
chapterbody 17: Amitabha Buddha on the left side.
chapterbody 18: Skanda and Sangharama Dharmapalas at the left and right.
chapterbody 20: On the offering table, there was also another statue, “Avalokitesvara in Royal Ease Posture,” that was made with vaidurya glass. This Avalokitesvara statue had the blue and green colors mixed together. It was very dignified and pretty. It carried the Goddess’ merciful light which was as vast as a big ocean.
chapterbody 22: Just at that moment, I heard a sound:
chapterbody 24: “Grand Master Lu, you can recite as well.”
chapterbody 26: I searched and found the source of the sound. Wow! The Avalokitesvara Bodhisattva on the offering table had already transformed and enlarged to fill up the whole main hall, like the blowing-bubbles - transparent and colorful, both illusory and real,.
chapterbody 28: I asked, “Recite which sutra?”
chapterbody 30: The Bodhisattva said, “Recite along.”
chapterbody 32: I said, “Oh Bodhisattva! I have learned Pu Men Pin before, and I am able to sing and recite, but as I do not have the sutra text with me today, how can I recite it? I know High King Avalokitesvara Sutra and I know it by heart; I will just recite High King Sutra then!”
chapterbody 34: The Bodhisattva nodded.
chapterbody 36: I also asked, “Where do I recite the sutra?”
chapterbody 38: The Bodhisattva said, “You go to the right wing. There is a little library and nobody is in there at this moment. You can sit in the library and recite the sutra!”
chapterbody 40: I asked, “To whom shall I recite?”
chapterbody 42: The Bodhisattva answered, “You will know.”
chapterbody 44: I asked, “How many times should I recite the sutra?”
chapterbody 46: The Bodhisattva replied, “A hundred times.”
chapterbody 48: I said, “A hundred times? You want me to recite until my mouth is dried and lips are burnt, and I have not had lunch yet! Please forgive me! Reciting for twenty times is good enough!”
chapterbody 50: The Bodhisattva did not reply, and disappeared with a smile.
chapterbody 53: During the time of reciting, I paid no attention to people who came in; even if people questioned me, I would not answer. I only wanted to complete this arduous task of reciting as soon as possible. I heard someone say, “This person is mumbling in his mouth, he could be insane.” But, no one kicked me away. I was silently moaning about the hardship in my mind.
chapterbody 54: That night, I slept.
chapterbody 56: I saw one hundred people kneeling in front of my bed.
chapterbody 57: I asked, “What are you doing? What are you doing?”
chapterbody 59: The hundred people said, “Many thanks to Grand Master Lu for delivering the one hundred of us from the sea of bitterness of the netherworld.”
chapterbody 61: “What?”
chapterbody 62: The hundred people replied, “Each time Grand Master Lu recited the sutra, one white lotus emanated from your mouth. We rode on the lotuses and left the sea of bitterness!”
chapterbody 67: On that day, I quietened my mind and recited High King Sutra for a hundred times. Even though each High King Sutra is short, reciting a hundred times would take a rather long time. I sat in the library for a total of six hours in order to complete reciting a hundred times. (Five minutes was taken for each time; the more I recited the faster it went.)
chapterbody 69: I felt deep veneration for him and immediately protected him for his trip to take up the new post.

chapterbody: n the evening of the 23rd of November 2011, I saw a person come to the front of my bed in my dream. This person was wearing yellow khaki pants and a white top. He had sparse salt and pepper hair. His face was quite slim and angular. As I could not recognize him, I asked him, “Who are you?”
chapterbody 3: He answered, “Yang Risong.”
chapterbody 5: I said, “Yang Risong? The name sounds familiar, but I still do not know who you are.”
chapterbody 7: He replied, “Forensic Doctor Yang Risong.”
chapterbody 9: At this time I quickly remembered. Forensic Doctor Yang Risong, a famous autopsy expert in the Ministry of Justice, is very well-known. There were lots of rumors about him, and he had exposed the truth of many mysterious cases.
chapterbody 11: I asked, “May I know why you are looking for me?”
chapterbody 13: He replied, “I need you to accompany me on my journey!”
chapterbody 15: “What happened to you?”
chapterbody 17: “Died!”
chapterbody 19: “What was the illness?”
chapterbody 21: “Colorectal cancer.”
chapterbody 23: I asked, “Why do you need me to accompany you?”
chapterbody 25: He replied, “I have to go to Japan to take up a new post, and have to pass over the ocean, but there are karmic creditors in the ocean who will hinder me. I need to rely on Grand Master Lu’s Buddhadharma for protection. You know that when I saved one party, the other party would surely be offended. I was a forensic doctor, and naturally these kinds of things will happen.”
chapterbody 27: “Why do you go to Japan?”
chapterbody 29: He replied, “I have affinity with Japan. I am taking the position of City God Territory Master in Japan.”
chapterbody 31: I felt deep veneration for him and immediately protected him for his trip to take up the new post.
chapterbody 33: Forensic Doctor Yang Risong of Taiwan actually became the City God Territory Master of Japan.
chapterbody 35: Along the way, even though there were resentful spirits, all were just unreasonable disturbances. I protected Yang Risong using three lights, and not even one thing happened.
chapterbody 37: Yang Risong said, “Once, after examining a corpse and determining that it was suicidal; on the way home, the deceased’s spirit rushed to pat my shoulder. I immediately returned to examine the corpse again, and I actually identified a suspicious point, and finally confirmed it to be homicidal. This was a tricky homicidal case, and it was purposely misrepresented as suicidal. If the corpse was not carefully inspected, one would not be able to tell otherwise.”
chapterbody 39: Yang Risong said, “I was the confidential secretary of Lord Yama in my previous life, and therefore I was born in this human world as a forensic doctor. Lord Yama once even invited me for dinner!”
chapterbody 41: Yang Risong said, “There was another time when I was examining a person who committed suicide by lying across railway tracks. The initial diagnosis was suicide on the railway tracks. Later on, when it was discovered that the deceased spirit was chasing the train for taking his life, I then realized that it was another homicidal case.”
chapterbody 43: I asked, “You can see as well?”
chapterbody 45: He replied, “I can see, there are gods and spirits.”
chapterbody 47: I asked, “Any wrongly accused?”
chapterbody 49: He replied, “Certainly, but then, as there are gods and spirits, there will not be any mistakes. A forensic doctor’s responsibility is really heavy.”
chapterbody 51: I asked, “You got the disease of livor mortis [purplish red discoloration of the skin]?”
chapterbody 52: He replied, “I had too much contact with the corpses, and got infected.”
chapterbody 55: I accompanied Yang Risong on his journey, and walked for a long distance. When I woke up, I was actually still in my bed.
chapterbody 58: Garuda received Buddha’s edict and became one of Buddhism’s protectors.
chapterbody 63: When Yang Risong arrived at the Japanese territory, his whole person was completely changed — he wore a big red robe and a crown. His facial features also changed to rounded appearance and a look of righteousness permeated.

chapterbody: ia Lou Da (Garuda), is the Sanskrit name of this God and it has been translated to different Chinese names like “Jia Liu Luo,” “Jia Lou Luo,” “Jia Luo Luo,” “Golden-Winged Bird,” “Wonderful-Winged Bird,” “Great Peng Golden-Winged Bird,” and so on.
chapterbody 3: This God in Hinduism has limitless power; it is a very fierce big bird, and is the mount of “Lord Vishnu.”
chapterbody 5: The bird form Garuda.
chapterbody 6: Angry eyes.
chapterbody 7: Sharp beak.
chapterbody 8: Both claws grasping various dragons.
chapterbody 9: The wings are made from different treasures.
chapterbody 10: Both wingspan stretch to several thousand miles long.
chapterbody 12: Legend has it that:
chapterbody 14: When the wings move, it can raise a typhoon.
chapterbody 15: The thrones of various Gods vibrate.
chapterbody 16: Garudas eat dragons as their food.
chapterbody 17: The emblem of Indonesia is “Garuda” — it is their national symbol. It originated from Hinduism’s Great Roc Golden-Winged Bird.
chapterbody 19: The mantra of this God:
chapterbody 21: “Om, jia-xi-bo, so-ha.”
chapterbody 23: Mudra:
chapterbody 25: The thumbs of both hands are crossed, the remaining four fingers form wings facing outward and wave three times like flying.
chapterbody 29: When Shakyamuni Buddha was living in this world, the “Naga” dragon family begged the Buddha, “Our Dragon family is already going to be eaten up by Garuda, we pray to Buddha to save our lives!”
chapterbody 31: Therefore, Buddha summoned Garuda and the dragon family to have a reconciliatory meeting.
chapterbody 33: Garuda said, “If we do not eat dragons, what else can we eat?”
chapterbody 35: The dragons were scared to death after hearing that. The dragon family said, “If you continue eating us, the dragon population will be exterminated, and then, what can the Garuda race eat in the future?”
chapterbody 37: Once these words came out, Garuda was stunned.
chapterbody 39: Therefore, Buddha said, “Starting from today, I will grant an edict to all Buddhist disciples. When performing the vast nectar transformation into food, Garuda will be the first one to be invoked to eat the cloud-like nectar, and this nectar is tasty, filling, and purifying. What do you think?”
chapterbody 41: Garuda was very satisfied after eating it. Thereupon, the three parties came to an agreement. Garuda then did not eat the dragon family, and switched to eating the “cloud-like nectar.”
chapterbody 43: Garuda received Buddha’s edict and became one of Buddhism’s protectors.
chapterbody 45: The sharp beak could expel the devils.
chapterbody 46: The claws could seize devils.
chapterbody 47: The wings could stir up the devils’ throne.
chapterbody 48: The golden wings could grant fortune to sentient beings because they are made with seven precious jewels.
chapterbody 50: When Grand Master Lu performs the noon-time offering of transforming nectar into food:
chapterbody 53: Great Peng Golden-Winged Bird
chapterbody 54: Raksasa and Hariti
chapterbody 55: All filled up with nectar
chapterbody 56: Om, mu-di-li, so-ha. Om, mu-di-li, so-ha. Om, mu-di-li, so-ha.
chapterbody 57: Sprinkle the nectar outwards.
chapterbody 59: There was one time, I saw the sun was suddenly covered up. The sky turned grey and the earth became dark, and a whirlwind ensued. I was greatly startled by the instant change.
chapterbody 61: I closed my eyes, unable to open them. When I opened my eyes, the sun was bright in the sky, and there was no sign of strong wind. What happened to the bright sun that was covered up?
chapterbody 62: I asked the spirit officer on duty.
chapterbody 63: All the ghosts and spirits of the wilderness
chapterbody 67: The recitation is:
chapterbody 68: The spirit officer on duty replied, “It was the arrival of Garuda.”
chapterbody 69: The first time my mother, Lu Yunu, made offerings to Garuda, she was given a ride by Garuda to tour the world. She was exhilarated.
chapterbody 70: Garuda has the great transcendental ability of going anywhere at will and traveling around the world at only a single thought!

chapterbody: disciple by the surname Han came from overseas to Taiwan Lei Tsang Temple to see me. He handed me a piece of paper filled up with written simplified Chinese words. Its content was as follows:
chapterbody 3: Disciple Han, divorced and was raising three children. After the divorce, he received two million American dollars.
chapterbody 5: He had a very close friend. This friend and his wife ran a jewelry business and had several shops. This couple were not only friends to him, but were former classmates and neighbors as well. They were very good friends who would frequently travel or have meals together and have heart-to-heart talks. Their friendship spanned more than thirty years.
chapterbody 7: The couple wanted to open another shop and invited him to join the partnership. He joyfully agreed, and by way of “lending,” gave the two million American dollars to this couple with whom he had thirty years of friendship. In the first couple of years, interests were given to him.
chapterbody 9: Later on, interest had to be temporarily owed; as owing accumulated, they decided to stay away. After chasing them several times for repayment, it ended with no results. Without any clues, he resorted to hire a lawyer to serve them a legal letter to claim what they owed.
chapterbody 11: From then on, the couple broke off contact with disciple Han. Even meeting up with them was impossible. (The couple simply moved out.)
chapterbody 12: Disciple Han sorrowfully related to me that he had sunk into dire straits. He could not afford living expenses, as well as his three children’s living and education expenses.
chapterbody 14: He said, “I had never doubted him as we had thirty years of friendship. He did not even write an IOU. The only evidence was the original cheque. But now, my family financial situation is getting very tight, and I do not know how to deal with it?”
chapterbody 16: I was quite deeply moved after I heard this. I said, “You have to discuss with your lawyer regarding any legal questions. The lawyer has more legal knowledge in this aspect. As for my part, I will draw a talisman and give it to you. This is a talisman for claiming debts. You take the talisman and together with joss paper, burn them at the Earth Deity temple in the afternoon of a “Success” day. It may help.”
chapterbody 18: The talisman style that I drew was as follows:
chapterbody 22: According to what I understood:
chapterbody 23: Once the Earth Wealth Deity received the decree, he would not dare to disregard it because it was assigned by Grand Master Lu to take the decree to the temple of Dongyue Emperor.
chapterbody 25: Dongyue Emperor was furious, “Ox-Head and Horse-Face, quickly arrest the souls of the couple here!”
chapterbody 27: Then this couple, both simultaneously dreamed of “Ox-Head” and “Horse-Face,” who in possession of arrest warrants, brought the couple to Dongyue Emperor.
chapterbody 29: Dongyue Emperor was as awe-inspiring as the mountain; he had a black face, round and wide-opened eyes. He was so powerful and stern-looking that people dared not look closely. The couple knelt down.
chapterbody 31: In the surrounding were ghost kings, ghost soldiers, Ox-Head, Horse-Face, Seventh Lord, and Eighth Lord, each carrying an instrument of torture, scaring the couple to death.
chapterbody 33: Dongyue Emperor roared out loudly, like the thunder in the sky. This couple immediately knocked their foreheads to the ground continuously like pounding garlic. Instantly, they confessed the whole matter.
chapterbody 35: Dongyue Emperor said, “After returning to the living world, you have to immediately return both principal and interest. Otherwise, you will be punished to the Great Avici Hell.”
chapterbody 37: The jailer took the couple to see the Great Avici Hell. They were so scared that they were trembling with fear and screamed continuously.
chapterbody 38: They kept saying, “We will not dare to do it again!”
chapterbody 40: The couple had the same dream and woke up at the same time with their whole bodies trembling in cold sweat. Thereupon, they quickly calculated the total amount of two million American dollars with interest, and delivered it to disciple Han on the same night.
chapterbody 42: The couple personally told disciple Han this matter, and disciple Han in return recommended them to promptly take refuge from Grand Master Lu because Grand Master Lu really has immeasurable spiritual power.
chapterbody 44: Disciple Han asked, “Who is Dongyue Emperor?”
chapterbody 46: I answered, “A great ruler deity in the Netherworld. He is the same as the Emperor of Mount Tai.”
chapterbody 48: This matter happened on November 26, 2011.

chapterbody: had written a book and its name was [Divination to Wow You]. This book was not meant to brag. Exactly how accurate was it? It was good enough as long as you, I, and he knew. It was actually not necessary to brag.
chapterbody 2: Mr. Zeng Haitang of Zhong Tian television program division raised his big thumb and said, “Really accurate!”
chapterbody 3: But worldly people are always boastful and arrogant. Nowadays it is rather difficult to find someone who is humble.
chapterbody 5: All the worldly people are:
chapterbody 7: All the articles in the world belong to Sanjiang.
chapterbody 8: Sanjiang’s articles belong to my village.
chapterbody 9: My village’s articles belong to my brother.
chapterbody 10: My brother learned about writing articles from me.
chapterbody 12: Anyway, all modern divinators always claim that they are number one, and other people are number two. Are they accurate? Heaven knows.
chapterbody 14: Someone asked me, “Regarding the presidential election, do you know who will be elected?” (Dong suan - A term frequently used in Taiwan during election. Its meaning is “Electoral Victory”)
chapterbody 16: I answered, “I know.”
chapterbody 18: “Are you ironclad accurate?”
chapterbody 20: “Accurate!”
chapterbody 22: People asked, “How do you know it is accurate?”
chapterbody 24: I answered, “Mine is the answer from heaven.” (When I look up to the sky, the name of the president will appear in the sky.)
chapterbody 26: Every time I look up to the sky, it is always accurate. I only told a small number of people. When the results came out, every one of us knowingly smiled.
chapterbody 28: The recent news media fabricated a lot of stories about me, and said that I was performing divination on behalf of someone for the presidential election.
chapterbody 30: The news media denounced and laughed:
chapterbody 32: “Asking gods and ghosts rather than asking people?”
chapterbody 34: The media reporter made groundless accusations about me, and spouted nonsense.
chapterbody 36: As to whether anybody had consulted me, these were also all unrealistic talks.
chapterbody 38: The media asked me, “Did anybody consult you to perform divination for the presidential election?”
chapterbody 40: I replied, “No.”
chapterbody 42: The media asked, “Who will be elected?”
chapterbody 44: I replied, “I know, but, I am not telling you.”
chapterbody 46: (I secretly smiled in my mind. After all, the one who requested me to perform divination for who would be elected president was actually the media itself. I knew who would be elected, but of course I could not say who. It would create a great amount of chaos if I revealed it; moreover, if one party was identified, the other party would inevitably be offended.)
chapterbody 48: Moreover, as an “outsider” monk and cultivator like me, I do not gain any benefit should a big matter like this spread. As a true cultivator, the worldly matters would automatically require the worldly people to manage. I did not want to be well known in this world because of this.
chapterbody 49: Actually, I am already quite famous. If I announced it, it would be a big problem.
chapterbody 50: Sheng-yen Lu
chapterbody 51: Someone asked, “Do you really know who will be elected president?”
chapterbody 52: I replied, “I know.”
chapterbody 55: I replied, “Heaven knows! Ha ha.”
chapterbody 58: And I also laughed, “Ha ha.”
chapterbody 60: The worldly people also laughed, “Ha ha.”
chapterbody 63: Someone asked, “Who is it?”
chapterbody 64: The person who asked the question laughed, “Ha ha.”
chapterbody 65: This article was written on November 27, 2011. According to the presidential election poll, the difference between the two candidates was 0.4 percent, the difference was so small that it intensified the election atmosphere.
chapterbody 66: I remembered three months ago, the poll between the two presidential candidates still had a 20 percent difference! At that time, I had said “tight”!

chaptername: 028 The Dim Light of the Long Corridor
chapterbody: hile I was in Taiwan, the weather in winter was cold. Around ten o’clock at night, I would usually walk on foot from the seventh phase to the fifth phase of the residential district. The walk took about forty minutes.
chapterbody 3: During the journey, I would exercise and chant.
chapterbody 5: Walking from the seventh phase to the fifth phase required passing through several high-rise buildings of the seventh phase, several sections of traffic lights, three to five small lanes, and three to four big streets. I tried to walk on the pedestrian path as much as possible.
chapterbody 7: In fact, it was impossible to walk mainly on the pedestrian path because some of the pedestrian paths were blocked by business signs.
chapterbody 9: Blocked by the parked scooters.
chapterbody 10: Blocked by the parked cars.
chapterbody 11: Blocked by the “voltage transformer.”
chapterbody 12: Blocked by the construction work.
chapterbody 13: Blocked by the big tree.
chapterbody 15: The newly-paved pedestrian paths were fairly pleasant to walk on; while the old paths were full of pot-holes and uneven, requiring one to walk with one foot higher than the other.
chapterbody 17: I frequently had to take a detour and walk on the asphalt road surface for scooters. All these made me cautiously walk for forty minutes.
chapterbody 19: I was used to taking “Shizheng North 2nd Road.” After passing by several tall buildings, I made a turn from “Shizheng North 2nd Road,” passed through a long corridor of a building, and then arrived at another big avenue. The light of this long corridor was dimmed at ten o’clock at night. This long corridor was rather long. It was a corridor of a building. It was very quiet with no one there; I quietly walked along this long corridor. I had been walking there for quite a while and had never encountered anybody walking on this long corridor.
chapterbody 21: This long corridor belonged to one particular building. There were sculptures, high and big chandeliers, and very flat and smooth ground tiles. This section of the road made me feel very comfortable and happy.
chapterbody 23: Originally, I was walking alone. Later on, the more I walked, the more I found something wrong. I could feel there were two adults and three children nearby following my footsteps.
chapterbody 25: All along, I am bold and skillful. Hence, I did not bother.
chapterbody 26: I was not even afraid. I walked mine, and they walked theirs.
chapterbody 27: While I was walking on this long corridor, I started with chanting the Buddha’s epithet, or reciting [High King Avalokiteshvara Sutra], and at the end chanting “Amitabha Rebirth Mantra”…
chapterbody 29: These five spirits, initially were:
chapterbody 31: Walking behind me.
chapterbody 32: Walking around me.
chapterbody 34: Later on, they were transformed to white stripes of light shadows dancing in front of me, just like five stripes of colored ribbons flying in the space.
chapterbody 36: I did not chant or form any mudra to get rid of them. They also did not have any evil intentions towards me. We felt comfortable being together.
chapterbody 38: Later on they raised a question, “This cultivator, who are you? You radiate light from your body.”
chapterbody 40: I replied, “I am Grand Master Lu.”
chapterbody 42: They said, “Oh! It is really Grand Master Lu. We have heard a lot about you. You have a well-known reputation, no wonder your body radiates rainbow light.”
chapterbody 44: I asked them back, “Who are you then?”
chapterbody 46: They replied, “In the original city planning, the seventh phase was a piece of deserted land with grass and weeds overgrown everywhere, and groups of stray dogs wandered around. In the middle, there were several ancient gravesites. We were one of the gravesites in which two adults and three children were buried; we were all grave-guarding ghosts. Later on, when the seventh phase was developed, many buildings were built, the graves and bones were completely dug up and dispersed, and we became the building-guarding ghosts. Originally, we shuttled among the empty buildings. After you came, we listened to your chanting of Buddhas’ name, of mantras, and of sutras. Your light and energy rubbed off on us and we could fly freely. From then, we could accumulate energy to form a body. We have to really thank you!”
chapterbody 48: I said, “It is a casual acquaintance, and it is our affinity, so there is no need to thank me.”
chapterbody 50: I also asked, “Where are you going in the future?”
chapterbody 53: I said, “Very good! I will accept you five ghosts to be my disciples. Follow me around, I will use my light to protect you all. Follow me on the path of cultivation!”
chapterbody 63: (I really took these five ghosts [as disciples].)
chapterbody 67: They replied, “We would like to follow Grand Master Lu to cultivate.”

chaptername: 029 The Great Compassion of White Dakini
chapterbody: received the empowerment of Vajrayana’s “Highest Yoga Tantra” from Master Thubten Dargye. After giving me the empowerment, Master immediately explained and taught me the pith instructions of the dharma practice.
chapterbody 3: The main points of his pith instructions were:
chapterbody 5: 1. Secret.
chapterbody 6: 2. Offering.
chapterbody 7: 3. Breathing.
chapterbody 8: 4. Wind Practice.
chapterbody 9: 5. Sensation.
chapterbody 10: 6. Happiness of emptiness.
chapterbody 11: 7. Realization.
chapterbody 13: Every time I visited the abode of Master Thubten Dargye, he repeatedly mentioned the importance of the pith instructions. He transmitted to me the Highest Yoga Tantra dharma practice without holding back anything. I could see that he placed high value on it.
chapterbody 15: Every time I visited “Jing Yim Fu Guo,” Master always asked me to go in first. Only three persons: Master, the attendant of Master, and I would be in the room privately. During that time, I was taught the important pith instructions.
chapterbody 17: After the dharma transmission was completed, then all my disciples were called to enter the room to pay respect to Great Grand Master Thubten Dargye.
chapterbody 19: In terms of theory and flavor of dharma, I have understood the “Highest Yoga Tantra.”
chapterbody 21: But, in practical cultivation, I do not have the “Karmamudra” of the human world. I truly tell everyone:
chapterbody 23: The “Karmamudra” of Vajrayana is the supreme of the unsurpassed expedient method.
chapterbody 24: It symbolizes the secret combination of “Wisdom” and “Expedient Method.”
chapterbody 25: Through the “Combination.”
chapterbody 26: Our “Sensations” have the greatest benefit.
chapterbody 27: Wisdom has grown.
chapterbody 28: Brightness has grown.
chapterbody 29: Great Happiness has grown.
chapterbody 31: Our “Mind” is staying within the broad clear sky and realizing the limitless “Self Nature.”
chapterbody 33: I had yogic response with “White Dakini.”
chapterbody 35: I formed the mudra to invocate:
chapterbody 37: “Interlocking both hands inwardly, two index fingers extended but not touching, only forming a circle, while the two thumbs point up side by side.”
chapterbody 38: (The two index fingers indicate the lotus leaves, the two thumbs are both inside the lotus flower.)
chapterbody 40: [The Mahavairocana Sutra Commentary] chapter ten states:
chapterbody 42: “White is the Bodhi Mind. Staying in this Bodhi Mind, is the dwelling place of White Dakini. This Bodhi Mind is the Buddha’s Bodhi where all Buddhas are frequently born, and is also the head of the Padma Family of Buddhas.”
chapterbody 44: From here, I use “Secret Words” to express as follows:
chapterbody 46: White Dakini, charming and graceful, without any false pretense, she is dignified with fragrant light as she descends.
chapterbody 48: A great white lotus flower appears in the space, its petals and pistils are soft, clean, and purified.
chapterbody 50: The body and mind of the cultivator, as if illusionary and transformed, enters into the center of the great white lotus flower.
chapterbody 51: The body and mind of White Dakini, as if illusionary and transformed, is also in the center of the great white lotus flower.
chapterbody 52: The bloomed great white lotus flower, gradually tightens up its lotus petals to form a lotus bud. (Unopened lotus flower.)
chapterbody 54: Both light drops dissolved.
chapterbody 55: Both qi energies opened up.
chapterbody 56: Both channels connected.
chapterbody 57: From bottom to crown.
chapterbody 58: Happiness and emptiness circulate interchangeably. (Profound meditation.)
chapterbody 60: At this time, great happiness, bright light, and empty nature all appear. With complete wisdom, bright light and rainbow are intertwining, thusness and the Buddha-nature are witnessed.
chapterbody 63: Both orifices merged.
chapterbody 64: White Dakini takes all the attainment, and empowers it into the whole body of the cultivator, from crown to feet.
chapterbody 65: This kind of state, does not exist in this “human world.” That kind of great sensation experienced transcends beyond all transcendences; indeed all the Buddhas are really born from the Bodhi Mind of White Dakini.
chapterbody 66: In my Vajrayana practice, I succeeded the stages:
chapterbody 69: Conduct or Performance Tantra.

chaptername: 030 Did They Not Come to Receive the Offerings?
chapterbody: he Offering Practice of Vajrayana is an important component in Vajrayana cultivation. There are even masters saying Offering is the focal point of Vajrayana.
chapterbody 3: Lineage Master Tsongkhapa said, “The three great attainments in Vajrayana are: 1. The attainment of Mantra Chanting Accomplishment (Chi Ming). 2. The attainment of Homa (Fire Offering). 3. The attainment of Samadhi.”
chapterbody 5: One of them is Homa (Fire Offering), which is the Offering Practice.
chapterbody 6: There are various types of Offering Practice in Vajrayana. I will not explain them one by one here. There are external offering, internal offering, secret offering, double secret offering (Thusness Offering), etc.
chapterbody 8: I always perform offerings when I have regular meals. That is, I offer to:
chapterbody 10: My Masters
chapterbody 11: Personal Deities
chapterbody 12: Vajra Protectors
chapterbody 14: During my offerings, I certainly know whether “they” have come and accepted the offerings or not.
chapterbody 16: This “knowing” comes in different forms:
chapterbody 18: 1. Seeing with one’s own eyes.
chapterbody 19: 2. Hearing it.
chapterbody 20: 3. Telepathy.
chapterbody 22: First of all, we have to “invocate,” then “blow qi,” and lastly “chant” (form mudra). We visualize the transformation of all the offering items into uncountable infinite numbers—they are limitless like clouds.
chapterbody 23: Generally speaking, whenever I perform the “Offering Practice,” I always truly know that masters, personal deities, and vajra protectors have descended and received my offerings. This is absolutely not a “Tiger Egg” (a Taiwanese slang for fabricated exaggeration).
chapterbody 25: One day, in October 2011, at “Rainbow Temple,” I was performing offering first before I had lunch.
chapterbody 27: I invocated masters, personal deities, and vajra protectors to descend. After the first invocation, I had no response.
chapterbody 28: For the second invocation, they also did not come to receive the offerings.
chapterbody 29: For the third invocation, they did not come.
chapterbody 31: I have always invocated three times, and usually they will come.
chapterbody 32: If they come after just one invocation, it indicates joy.
chapterbody 33: If they come after two invocations, it indicates average.
chapterbody 34: If they come after three invocations, it indicates the offerings are too ordinary.
chapterbody 36: But, it is very rare to have a no-show after three invocations.
chapterbody 37: At that time, since they did not show up after three invocations, I then realized there was some problem. If the various venerables did not come to accept the offerings, then there must be something the matter with the food.
chapterbody 39: I carefully tasted the food. When I tried the plate of “or-ah!” [a favorite oyster dish of the Taiwanese; the term is used to refer to a delightful local dish consisting of oysters fried in eggs served with sweet sauce], I realized that the oysters had turned rotten and stale.
chapterbody 40: No wonder the masters, personal deities, and vajra protectors did not come to receive the offerings.
chapterbody 42: I told Master Lianyin, who then instructed Reverend Lianwan, to pay special attention when opening the “or-ah.” “Or-ah” have shells. The reverends opened the shells themselves.
chapterbody 44: There was another occasion, and everyone knows about this occasion!
chapterbody 46: A group of us went to visit Buddha’s Eight Sacred Sites, located at the five-thousand-year-old city “Wa Na Na Xi” (Varanasi) on the banks of the Ganges River in India. This old city is the holy land of Hinduism. Many Hindu temples such as the temples of Brahma (God of Creation), Shiva (God of Destruction), and Vishnu (God of Protection) are all located here.
chapterbody 48: Brahma.
chapterbody 49: Shiva.
chapterbody 50: Vishnu.
chapterbody 53: That day, we took breakfast early in the morning.
chapterbody 54: I invocated three times, and each time, the masters, personal deities, and vajra protectors did not come to receive offerings. No matter how I invocated, they did not come.
chapterbody 56: I became anxious and specially invocated again with “Heart within Heart” [mudra], but still they did not arrive.
chapterbody 57: I skipped that morning’s breakfast.
chapterbody 59: There were eighteen of us who were at the breakfast table. Later on, all the seventeen fellow disciples who ate breakfast collapsed with “La Xi” [diarrhea] and the whole group rushed to the washroom. The diarrhea was very severe.
chapterbody 61: We were joking around and said, “We are finished with La Xi!” (Wa Na Na Xi) [The Chinese pronunciation of Varanasi, “Wa Na Na Xi,” sounds like the phrase “finished with diarrhea!”]
chapterbody 62: Do you not say it is accurate?
chapterbody 67: In Varanasi, [the gods] carry an aura of extraordinary mysticism.

chaptername: 031 God of Wealth and Merit Advised Me to Buy a House
chapterbody: round the year of 2000, I lived in seclusion in Taichung, Taiwan. At that time, the seventh phase of the city planning district in Taichung was a desolate piece of land with overgrown weeds and wild dogs gathered together, an uninhabited countryside phenomenon.
chapterbody 2: On that piece of land, there were several erected buildings and their names were:
chapterbody 5: Tian Xi.
chapterbody 6: Guoji Yinyue Ting.
chapterbody 7: Renwen Lingyu.
chapterbody 8: Xin Xue Li.
chapterbody 9: Lixing Ganxing.
chapterbody 11: Besides these six buildings, a big piece of land was still vacant. At that time, as soon as night fell, it became an unpatrolled dead-end and a heaven for wild dogs.
chapterbody 13: One time, I distributed newspapers (True Buddha News Weekly), and magazines (Enlightenment Magazine) to the Earth Deity temples in the neighborhood.
chapterbody 15: (I have a habit of collecting the already-read True Buddha News Weekly and Enlightenment Magazines. After they have accumulated for a while, I will carry and place them at the Earth Deity temples in the surrounding areas, waiting for any sentient being with the affinity to take them home to read. This is also one of the ways to save sentient beings.)
chapterbody 17: Once, as usual, I went to the Earth Deity temples again to distribute newspapers and magazines.
chapterbody 19: I arrived at the Earth Deity temple of “Taichung Seventh Phase”; this Earth Deity temple was built on a sunken land like the shape of a seashell. (I called it seashell cave.)
chapterbody 21: The God of Wealth and Merit actually manifested and said to me, “Grand Master Lu, you must buy a house! Hurry! Hurry!”
chapterbody 23: I replied, “This is a desolate piece of land not suitable for residential purpose.”
chapterbody 25: The God of Wealth and Merit said again, “You must buy a house! Listen to me, you can’t go wrong.”
chapterbody 27: I answered, “I have no money.”
chapterbody 29: The God of Wealth and Merit said, “Gather together.”
chapterbody 31: In my mind, I did not plan to be a long-term resident in Taiwan; I had to return to Seattle, United States. I was only living in seclusion temporarily in Taiwan; hence, I was not persuaded.
chapterbody 33: The God of Wealth and Merit said, “You take a look! It will be like this in the future.”
chapterbody 35: I took a look, “Ha”! I was so shocked.
chapterbody 37: A scenery was revealed in front of me –
chapterbody 39: Buildings mushroomed one after another (they are countless); a scenery of prosperity and brilliance was displayed.
chapterbody 40: Splendid lights.
chapterbody 41: Magnificent and spectacular architecture.
chapterbody 42: High-rise blocks.
chapterbody 43: Wonderful, artistic and beautiful style.
chapterbody 44: All buildings are luxurious homes.
chapterbody 46: The God of Wealth and Merit said, “This seventh phase area of Taichung is equivalent to the Xinyi District of Taipei.”
chapterbody 48: I swallowed a mouthful of saliva and said, “Good enough! I will see.”
chapterbody 50: At that time, I looked through all six buildings. “Lian Ju Heping” was comparatively expensive at one hundred ninety thousand per ping [unit of area equivalent to approximately 3.3058 square meters (used in Japan and Taiwan)]. “Lixing Ganxing” was comparatively more expensive, though the difference was not much. The price of Tian Xi, Yinyue Ting, Renwen Lingyu, and Xin Xue Li were more or less the same.
chapterbody 53: I took out three coins — divination standard for accuracy; I faced towards the direction of the temple and requested help from the Earth Deity. I asked if it was good to buy or not.
chapterbody 54: Three positive responds [a sacred combination indicating affirmative] confirmed that I could proceed to purchase.
chapterbody 56: I showed the results of three positive responds to Shimu, Master Lianxiang, to prove to her that the God of Wealth and Merit really told me to buy the house.
chapterbody 57: Eventually we bought the house! Based on the price in year 2000, the selling price in year 2010 had risen five times. (Within ten years, the price had gone up five times.)
chapterbody 59: I am very thankful to the God of Wealth and Merit who actually told me to buy the house. Although the God of Wealth and Merit is the lowest-grade god according to the divinity ranks, yet, small gods can also achieve big merit.
chapterbody 61: His words are very accurate!
chapterbody 67: When I stood in front of one of those buildings, the broker urged us to buy.
chapterbody 68: I am a cultivator and shall not kill.
chapterbody 69: Sheng-yen Lu

chapterbody: hen I was living in the United States, there was a period of time I stayed in the mountain area. Let us call it, “South Mountain!” It was a small community of about fifty households and most of them were Caucasians. Considerately The houses were not considered small, as every house was about six thousand square feet in size. The houses were big and each had its own architectural style. It could be considered a famous community.
chapterbody 3: When we were living there, everything was very cozy. We had sweet dreams and slept soundly. The environment was very serene and elegant. Opened my eyes, I could see an expanse of green in the front and at the backyard. We felt carefree and relaxed, especially with the quietude of the surroundings.
chapterbody 5: Initially, I discovered a few pieces of peanut shells in my storage room for blankets. I was very astonished. Who secretly ate peanuts here?
chapterbody 7: There was no need to suspect anyone because anyone could eat peanuts without being controlled, so there was no reason to hide in the storage room. Who was that?
chapterbody 9: Needless to say, it must be the “mice.” Especially so because mice droppings were found at the same location.
chapterbody 11: I am a cultivator and shall not kill. Initially, I did not pay any attention.
chapterbody 13: Once, while cultivating in front of the shrine, I was entering samadhi during the main section of the cultivation sadhana. The moment I closed my eyes, I heard the squeaking sound of mice. I opened my eyes and found two fluffy little things chasing each other between the gaps of the stair railing. The stair railing was upright, the two mice were dashing and dodging left and right in a S-shape pattern, and making squeaking sounds.
chapterbody 15: I looked at them. Both of them looked at me without any fear. The two small green-bean eyes looked around in an unrestrained manner.
chapterbody 17: I would normally disregard this kind of performance. But they were taking advantage of a Buddhist’s ethics of high tolerance, endurance, uprightness, and integrity as an opportunity of getting their way.
chapterbody 19: They intensified their lawlessness.
chapterbody 20: They built a nest in the sofa.
chapterbody 21: The defecated and urinated everywhere.
chapterbody 22: They behaved atrociously on the dining table.
chapterbody 23: They bit through the hot gas pipe.
chapterbody 24: They played hide-and-seek in the living room even in the daytime.
chapterbody 25: They bit through clothes.
chapterbody 26: They ferociously showed fangs and claws at people.
chapterbody 28: I thought, “If I ignore the mice, they will think that they can be self-indulgent. If there is no tiger on the mountain, the monkey becomes the king. If I do not have a cat in the house, the mice would become the boss.”
chapterbody 30: An American rodent control expert was called to come.
chapterbody 32: He said, “There are lots of mice in this mountain area. In the house located at the front of the mountain, I caught twenty-seven mice altogether at one time.”
chapterbody 34: I asked, “Were they alive or dead?”
chapterbody 36: He said, “Poison was used, and then we found a total of twenty-seven mice in the basement.”
chapterbody 38: I said, “Would it be possible to catch the mice alive and return them to other mountains?”
chapterbody 40: The rodent control expert said, “I don’t know how to catch them alive, they will die if poison is used. There is one type of poison that the mice like to take. They become very thirsty after taking it, and then they need to find water to drink. They will end up dying in the river ditch.”
chapterbody 42: I asked, “Is it possible to sprinkle a chemical substance that blocks them from entering?”
chapterbody 44: The expert said, “Mice are very clever, they can burrow holes in the ground to enter.”
chapterbody 46: Frankly speaking, except for not being able to open the refrigerator, the mice at my home had become lawless. They considered themselves kings, wreaking havoc in my humble home; a big group of small mice also followed behind [the kings], adding to the chaos and making so much noise.
chapterbody 48: What could be done?
chapterbody 50: Suddenly, I thought of “Jambhala” (Yellow God of Wealth). Was the hand of Yellow Jambahala to handle these “clamorous” mice.
chapterbody 53: “Jambhala” put down the treasure-spouting mongoose which drove all the clown mice out of my home.
chapterbody 54: Wow! Such strong spiritual efficacy!
chapterbody 56: The treasure-spouting mongoose got rid of all the arrogant mice, and my house has no more mice!
chapterbody 59: Vajra Ucchusma is extremely ferocious. He has immeasurable dharma power and an extra-ordinary dignified appearance. His light for removing filth is inconceivable.
chapterbody 61: Sheng-yen Lu
chapterbody 67: I invocated, “Om, jum-bah-lah, chan-lan-chah-nah-yeh, so-ha.”

chaptername: 033 Comments on Andy Lau from Head to Toe
chapterbody: n 2011, Andy Lau was given the best actor award in the Taipei Golden Horse Film Festival and Awards ceremony. My attention was therefore brought onto him. He started his career when he was young, and he was very popular even in his prime years. He is already fifty years old, and in the movie, television, and musical industries, he is doing well. It is very rare to have a person like this.
chapterbody 3: I comment based on his “Facial features”:
chapterbody 5: Eyebrows – crescent eyebrows.
chapterbody 6: Eye – eagle eyes.
chapterbody 7: Nose – hooked eagle nose.
chapterbody 8: Mouth – fish mouth.
chapterbody 9: Ear – rabbit ears.
chapterbody 11: The Siblings Palace is on the “eyebrows.” He is considered good at dealing with people, continuously cooperative, and has received lots of assistance from his benefactors.
chapterbody 13: The Sagacity Palace is on the “eyes.” He is considered smart and wise, and he has the ability to perceive what is going to happen.
chapterbody 15: The Wealth Palace is on the “nose.” People in general do not like to associate with people having this kind of nose because they are likely to bring harm to others.
chapterbody 17: Fortunately, his eagle eyes mitigated the defects from his eagle-hooked nose. As a result, his wealth has become better.
chapterbody 19: His “Mouth,” a fish mouth, is considered only average.
chapterbody 21: The Life Expectancy Palace is on the “ears.” His ears are shaped like those of a rabbit, and fans out a little. His lifespan is considered average, but he has to guard against accidents.
chapterbody 23: The Emotion Palace is behind the “eyebrows.” He is considered to be an affectionate person, but he is quite low-key; there are some rough patches which he will go through smoothly.
chapterbody 25: The Servant Palace is on the “chin.” Andy Lau’s chin is considered the worst type, for it tells of a fate that is not as good in his old age. He may lead a miserable life, and friends and relatives may desert him.
chapterbody 27: The Authority and Status Palace on the “smile lines” shows that he still has authority and status.
chapterbody 29: I was only briefly analyzing Andy Lau’s facial features, the strongest parts are his “eyes” and “nose,” and the worst one is his “chin.”
chapterbody 31: For this type of face, he could be popular for a certain period only, yet somehow his popularity is still high when he reached fifty years old. This is an “anomaly.”
chapterbody 33: This kind of face could also be that of an ordinary person, but he remains top in the entertainment performance business. This is also considered “atypical.” (He must work very hard.)
chapterbody 35: According to my observation, I classify this type of facial characteristic as “Karmic Hindrance Type.” It is the type with severe karmic hindrance. Based on the “Three Pavilions”:
chapterbody 37: Severe karmic hindrance.
chapterbody 38: From birth to teenager, not good. (Upper Pavilion) From hair root to eyebrow.
chapterbody 39: From teenager to prime years, not good. (Middle Pavilion) From eyebrow to nose tip. But he worked diligently and appreciated his work.
chapterbody 40: From prime years to old age, the most poor. (Lower Pavilion) From nose tip to lower chin.
chapterbody 41: These are perceived from the “three pavilions.” His three pavilions of old age, middle age, and youth are all not so good. (Short and wrinkled)
chapterbody 43: It was strange that:
chapterbody 45: Currently he has reached fifty years of age, but he is still very popular. This is puzzling. According to [the above] reasoning, he should be ill-fated all along; but at fifty years old, his timing, life, and fortune, are still pretty good in the eyes of ordinary people. He is not only pretty good, but also a person at the top, standing above the clouds.
chapterbody 47: Have his karmic hindrances been purified?
chapterbody 48: I was very curious.
chapterbody 49: I thought it over and over, but still could not figure it out.
chapterbody 51: One night, God Ucchusma appeared in my dream. This Vajra is the Great Sovereign Divine King Buddha, who is manifested from Shakyamuni Buddha’s heart.
chapterbody 52: He specializes in eradicating “filthy negative energy” and removing “hindrances.”
chapterbody 55: He voluntarily told me, “It is true that Andy Lau is a person originally carrying karmic hindrances, but he had endorsed the products of the toilet and bathroom fixtures manufacturer, HCG. His pictures are printed on the toilet facilities.”
chapterbody 58: To place one’s picture on the toilet long term is the “Practice of Eradicating Karmic Hindrances.” This is really amazing! (Using filth to remove karmic hindrance), his high popularity could possibly continue on.
chapterbody 59: It is up to you to believe it or not!
chapterbody 63: Vajra Ucchusma is extremely ferocious. He has immeasurable dharma power and an extra-ordinary dignified appearance. His light for removing filth is inconceivable.
chapterbody 64: Vajra Ucchusma also said, “Andy Lau’s merit comes from his not minding his pictures being placed on the toilets. Years of filthy negative energy eradicated all of Andy Lau’s karmic hindrances, turning ominousness to auspiciousness. Once the karmic hindrances were eliminated, great fortune descended and this is the reason why Andy Lau was able to hasten fortune and avoid calamity!”

chaptername: 034 Do Not Sell Fish These Few Days
chapterbody: went to the western harbor fish market. The moment I stepped in, I could smell the fishy stench—which is typical of any fish market everyone knows. I came not to buy fish, but to circumambulate the giant-sized fish market one time and chant the “Deliverance Mantra.”
chapterbody 3: Chanting mantra.
chapterbody 5: Visualizing.
chapterbody 7: All the souls of the fishes, prawns, and so on, were flying up; the whole space was filled with delivered spirits.
chapterbody 8: They covered the early rising red sun entirely.
chapterbody 10: I have a metaphor to describe this kind of deliverance scenario. It is like a movie, “Locusts in Transit,” that I have seen before. Imagine: thousands and thousands of locusts gathering like black clouds and completely covering the big sun. This is the way I delivered the fish family.
chapterbody 11: I walked round and round.
chapterbody 13: Suddenly, a fishmonger walked in front of me and said, “Grand Master Lu, why are you here?”
chapterbody 16: I asked, “You are?”
chapterbody 18: The fishmonger replied, “I am Lianhua Fuquan, your disciple.”
chapterbody 20: “Oh!” I said, as I didn’t expect to encounter a disciple amongst fishmongers in the fish market.
chapterbody 22: The fishmonger said, “Grand Master Lu, you come to buy fish. I have the freshest ones and I can offer live fish to you.”
chapterbody 24: I smiled and said, “I come to buy fish, but rather than buying visible fish, I want to buy invisible fish.”
chapterbody 25: The fishmonger was startled, “Are there any invisible fish?”
chapterbody 27: I replied, “There are. All sentient beings have [a spirit]; how could there be none?”
chapterbody 29: I was about to leave, but the fishmonger said, “Grand Master Lu, please leave me a piece of advice.”
chapterbody 31: I glanced at this fishmonger disciple, his glabella (the area between the eyebrows) was emanating blackness, I said, “Do not sell fish these few days, alright?”
chapterbody 33: The fishmonger disciple asked, “Which few days?”
chapterbody 35: I replied, “These three days.”
chapterbody 37: “Are there any issues?”
chapterbody 39: “I do not know!” I replied.
chapterbody 41: The fishmonger said, “Grand Master Lu, you nevertheless have to give me a reason. I can stop selling, I will definitely listen to you. However, it will be difficult for me to explain to my family if I do not run business for three days!”
chapterbody 43: I looked at his glabella emanating blackness. Hesitantly, I said, “It is a mystery!”
chapterbody 45: “What is the mystery?”
chapterbody 47: “I do not know!”
chapterbody 49: I had a laugh and left. I told the fishmonger disciple, “Chant the High King Sutra frequently to hasten auspiciousness and avoid ominousness; only then, calamities will be eradicated.”
chapterbody 51: The fishmonger replied, “I will chant when I am free!”
chapterbody 53: The fishmonger disciple did not follow my advice to stop selling fish for three days; on the second day, while lots of fish were sold, one of them was a puffer fish which a buyer brought home to cook. The whole family contracted food poisoning and was sent to the hospital for treatment.
chapterbody 55: The fishmonger disciple argued that he “absolutely did not know it was poisonous!”
chapterbody 58: He was plagued by the lawsuit that came about from the case of the “puffer fish” poisoning.
chapterbody 61: The fishmonger disciple regretted very much that he did not listen to Grand Master Lu’s advice right from the outset!
chapterbody 63: The police traced it to the fishmonger disciple.
chapterbody 64: However, everyone knows that “puffer fish” are poisonous. In general, the chefs at Japanese restaurants selling “puffer fish” know how to remove the poison, but an ordinary family may not know.
chapterbody 67: News came later:
chapterbody 68: I thought why not pray to the Mountain God and Earth God?
chapterbody 69: Sheng-yen Lu

chapterbody: hen I was in university, I studied “Surveying.” Therefore, I could read “topographic maps,” and I saw that the mountains of “Wufeng” are connected with the mountains of “Dakeng.”
chapterbody 3: Once, I went to the “Provincial Parliament” (at that time) of “Wufeng.” Suddenly, I had a strange idea. I actually thought that I could eventually reach Dakeng just by walking from the mountain at the back of the Provincial Parliament, towards the direction to Dakeng.
chapterbody 5: I thought I could do:
chapterbody 7: First, practice mountain climbing.
chapterbody 8: Second, exercise.
chapterbody 9: Third, study feng shui.
chapterbody 10: Fourth, determine my own physical strength.
chapterbody 11: Fifth, satisfy my curiosity.
chapterbody 13: I thought, in any case, those are not high mountain ranges, for they are only a few small hills. It cannot possibly be dangerous!
chapterbody 15: Alone, I went without carrying food or water, backpack or climbing gear. I was a surveyor before, and I had seen big mountains and small hills. These few small hills should not pose any big problem based on my robust feet work.
chapterbody 17: I thought I had very good eyesight, so as long as I walked northward, I should be able to reach “Dakeng.”
chapterbody 19: Hence, since action speaks louder than words, I started walking, certain that I must be able to reach “Dakeng.”
chapterbody 20: I followed a little path, and went into the mountain; at first, I could still identify the directions, and there were a few farmhouses belonging to the mountain residents. I felt reassured since there were people living here.
chapterbody 22: I therefore walked over a hill. And over another hill. And over another hill again. At this time, I felt something was not quite right.
chapterbody 24: Those little paths that were trekked out by people became tinier and tinier. Eventually, they became tiny trails. (Trails mean little path.)
chapterbody 26: After crossing over three small hills, I felt a bit out of breath. I felt extremely thirsty. Having only crossed over three hills, I looked around from the top of the hill—front, back, left, and right—and found that the hills were joined one after the other continuously with no end. They were the same all around. I walked from the human-cultivated dryland in the mountain to the uncultivated valley.
chapterbody 28: Startlingly I realized:
chapterbody 30: I completely lost my sense of direction — east, south, west, and north. Physical strength was sapping. The surrounding environment had been changed, I was trapped in the valley.
chapterbody 32: Mountains were in all directions, and I did not know which mountain was the one leading to “Dakeng.” If I continued walking forward regardless of the situation, would I be going deeper and deeper into the mountain? My mind became blank. I felt extremely frightened.
chapterbody 34: Once this kind of feeling sprung up, fear set in, and my field of vision was filled with just an expanse of forest. I cried out miserably! Although I was bold and skillful, yet this time, I admit that I could not possibly return home.
chapterbody 36: I was afraid to venture forward! But which direction would bring me back to the Provincial Parliament? I was already at the point of “having roamed over all the mountains and streams and suspected there was no way out,” and where will there be “another village” where is the light at the end of the tunnel?
chapterbody 38: I thought why not pray to the Mountain God and Earth God? I recited the Local Earth Deity Mantra three times:
chapterbody 40: “Namo, san-man-duo, mu-tuo-nan, om, du-lu-du-lu, di-wei, so-ha.”
chapterbody 42: My left leg stamped three times on the ground.
chapterbody 44: Oh no! There was no response at all. I was destined to die in the mountains.
chapterbody 46: Suddenly, I became aware that far away behind me was a big tree, and it seemed there was a person under the tree. I moved my body closer to the big tree, and an old man was really sitting under the tree smoking. He had bronze-colored skin; his upper body was naked and he was wearing a pair of black shorts.
chapterbody 48: I asked, “What is the way back to the Provincial Parliament?”
chapterbody 50: The old man replied, “To the right walk straight, you will see a small path. Follow that path and walk around the mountain for one and a half hours. You will return to the Provincial Parliament.”
chapterbody 53: I finally returned to level ground! Thanks to Heaven and Earth!
chapterbody 67: I felt ecstatic, walked a couple steps, and wanted to turn back round to thank the old man. When I turned back, where was that old man? Only two, three seconds, and the old man disappeared. Oh Heavens! Mountain God, Earth God, gods and spirits manifested!

chapterbody: ver since the initiation of my divine eyes, the number of buddhas and bodhisattvas that I have seen are innumerable. In particular, on the day when my divine eyes were initiated, from the “Four Enlightened [Realms]” to the “Six Non-enlightened [Realms],” I traveled the whole ten dharma realms once. Heavens! So it is actually like this, so it is actually like this.
chapterbody 3: I called myself “Padmakumara,” the reason of which comes from the fact that I had truly seen “Padmakumara.”
chapterbody 5: This was the basis on which I wrote two hundred and twenty-six books. If I, Grand Master Lu, utter any false words, then may the Taiwanese expression apply to me: “Yaoshou!” [It means, “Premature death.”]
chapterbody 7: Certainly, not only did I just see Padmakumara, but during samadhi, I also truly saw.
chapterbody 9: Is it an “illusion”? I say no.
chapterbody 10: Is it “self-deception”? I say no.
chapterbody 11: Is it a “shadow”? I say no.
chapterbody 12: Is it a “misperception”? I say no.
chapterbody 13: Is it an “abnormality”? I say no.
chapterbody 14: Is it a “crazy thought”? I say no.
chapterbody 15: Is it a “random thought”? I say no.
chapterbody 16: Am I “dreaming”? I say no.
chapterbody 17: I can only say, my seeing, is true seeing.
chapterbody 19: Here are several small examples!
chapterbody 21: First:
chapterbody 23: I was in Mainland China, and on the plane from Beijing to Taiyuan. During samadhi, I truly saw “Western Paradise Amitabha Buddha.” Words could hardly describe his “Hugeness,” “Magnificence,” and “Brilliance.”
chapterbody 25: It was extremely inconceivable!
chapterbody 27: In particular, his feet, which were stepping on the lotus flower, were extremely exquisite.
chapterbody 29: Amitabha Buddha said, “For every action, uphold the precepts, and one will then have peace.”
chapterbody 31: Second:
chapterbody 33: I was in Gyeongju City of South Korea to pay my respects to Medicine Vaidurya Light King Buddha. I performed the great homage with full-body prostrations.
chapterbody 35: On my return trip in the car, during samadhi, I truly saw Medicine Vaidurya Light King Buddha, Bhaisajyaraja Bodhisattva (Medicine King Bodhisattva), Bhaisajyasamudgata Bodhisattva (Medicine Superior Bodhisattva), Sunlight Bodhisattva, Moonlight Bodhisattva, and The Twelve Divine Generals of Medicine Buddha. Every venerable revealed an incomparably dignified golden body.
chapterbody 37: With ten thousand rays of radiant light.
chapterbody 38: A thousand strobes of auspicious energies.
chapterbody 40: Medicine Vaidurya Light King Buddha said, “Your sickness will be cured!”
chapterbody 42: At that time, I had the “four great dispersions,” and the “skull-splitting-into-eight-petals” phenomenon. I fully recovered later on. It really was a miracle.
chapterbody 44: Third:
chapterbody 46: I was in Taiwan. At that time, I had just encountered a matter that I was dragged into without any reason, it seemed there was no way out, with lots of difficulties.
chapterbody 48: During samadhi, I truly saw Namo Avalokitesvara Bodhisattva, who opened up a smile on her face.
chapterbody 49: The bodhisattva’s golden body and brightness, were so beautiful that nothing could describe it, surpassing everyone ever seen before, and no one could compare with her beauty.
chapterbody 51: Avalokitesvara Bodhisattva said to me, “Every matter has been resolved!”
chapterbody 52: It was on the same day that I saw the bodhisattva, the news came to us that everything had been resolved.
chapterbody 55: I was in Seattle in the United States. On the first day while expounding [The Great Exposition of Secret Mantra], during samadhi, I truly saw Lineage Master Tsongkhapa, Lineage Master of the Yellow Sect in Tibetan Buddhism (Gelugpa), appear in the space right above the front of my seat.
chapterbody 58: Secret
chapterbody 59: Soft
chapterbody 61: Energetic
chapterbody 62: Lineage Master Tsongkhapa said to me, “Blessing Grand Master Lu for expounding [The Great Exposition of Secret Mantra]!”
chapterbody 63: Fourth:
chapterbody 64: Lineage Master Tsongkhapa radiated brilliance that continuously blessed me softly and gently. This kind of brightness was indescribable.
chapterbody 68: (I gave these four small examples to showcase the very truth of the truth. After I attained the path of seeing, awakened and achieved enlightenment, I understood the arising and cessation of phenomena and the arising and cessation of the mind. Everything I understood thoroughly.)

chaptername: 037 Do Not Cling to the Inexistence of “Padmakumara”
chapterbody: here was detailed research on the caves of Dunhuang at Lanzhou University, In Mainland China. They written a book [The Dunhuang Story of Buddhism] which mentioned Padmakumara. It described two Padmakumaras in the time period of Golden Light Buddha, and the process by which they cultivated to become Bodhisattvas. One of the Padmakumaras was Avalokitesvara Bodhisattva, and the other Padmakumara was Mahasthamaprapta Bodhisattva.
chapterbody 3: If you were to go to Dunhuang and ask the administrator, “Which cave has Padmakumara inside?”
chapterbody 5: The administrator would answer, “Every cave has Padmakumara.”
chapterbody 7: If you were to ask, “Within Cave No. 314, are all Padmakumara?”
chapterbody 9: The administrator would answer, “Yes.”
chapterbody 11: In Taiwan, Zhang Mingcong, who is interested in researching on the history of Buddhism, presented more evidence. He listed various Padmakumaras, complete with pictures and narratives. He said, “There are uncountable Padmakumaras.”
chapterbody 13: I often explain like this:
chapterbody 15: The [Amitabha Sutra[ states, “In the west, there are ten trillion Buddha-lands. Amongst these, there is a Buddha named Amitabha.”
chapterbody 17: Let us think about it:
chapterbody 19: Ten trillion Buddha-lands. That is ten trillion Buddha fields (Pure Lands).
chapterbody 21: For every Buddha-land there is a dwelling Buddha. Do you know the epithets of the Buddhas in the ten trillion Buddha-lands?
chapterbody 23: In High King Sutra:
chapterbody 25: Fifteen hundred Buddhas – what are their epithets?
chapterbody 26: Fifteen thousand Buddhas – what are their epithets?
chapterbody 27: All the countless Buddhas – what are their epithets?
chapterbody 28: Can Buddhist disciples count all the buddha epithets in the buddha pure lands?
chapterbody 30: And:
chapterbody 32: Still again, for people who cling on to the eighth consciousness, “Tathagatagarbha Consciousness” (Alaya Consciousness), the six sutras of the Consciousness-only school of Buddhism are:
chapterbody 34: Samdhinirmocana Sutra
chapterbody 35: Avatamsaka Sutra
chapterbody 36: Lankavatara Sutra
chapterbody 37: Ghanavyuha Sutra
chapterbody 38: Mahayana-Abhidharma Sutra
chapterbody 39: The Appearance of Tathagata Sutra
chapterbody 40: (The above are the Six Sutras of Consciousness-only.)
chapterbody 42: In fact, in the research on the Consciousness-only school, various knowledgeable teachers of Buddhist studies, have many different ways of connoting Consciousness-only:
chapterbody 44: One consciousness connotation – of the Satyasiddhi Sect.
chapterbody 45: Two consciousness connotation – in the Awakening of Faith in the Mahayana.
chapterbody 46: Three consciousness connotation – in the Lankavatara Sutra.
chapterbody 47: Five consciousness connotation – in the Awakening of Faith in the Mahayana.
chapterbody 48: Six consciousness connotation – in the Abhidharma Mahavibhasa Sastra, Abhidharmakosa Sastra, Treatise on the Ability to Quantify (Nengliang Lun), Pramanavarttika.
chapterbody 49: Eight consciousness connotation – of the Consciousness-only Sect (Yogacara School).
chapterbody 50: Nine consciousness connotation – in the Mahayana-samgraha-sastra.
chapterbody 51: Ten consciousness connotation – in the Moheyan Lun (Commentary on the Awakening of Faith).
chapterbody 52: (There is even the connotation of [one mind is] limitless consciousness, in the Garbhadhatu Treatise of Eastern Esoteric Buddhism.)
chapterbody 53: (Void Consciousness connotation – in Sheng-yen Lu’s Treatise.)
chapterbody 54: I personally feel that the eighty-four thousand teachings of the Tathagata are for treating the eighty-four thousand afflictions of sentient beings. They are completely not cast in stone to adhere to one rigid format; it is all about prescribing the right medicine for a specific illness. Hence, the right teaching at the right time reflects the unobstructed and great expedient wisdom of Buddhism.
chapterbody 56: It is also wrong to only understand and cling to “the Eighth Consciousness.” Mahayana Buddhism is fundamentally an immeasurable expedient means that is unobstructed and non-self.
chapterbody 57: Someone asked Reverend Zhaozhou, “What is Zhaozhou?”
chapterbody 59: Reverend Zhaozhou replied, “East gate, west gate, south gate, north gate.” (That really means no obstruction, no-self, immeasurableness, and no attachment.)
chapterbody 61: I personally have witnessed Padmakumara and Lotus Light Self-Mastery Buddha. If you say they do not exist, that means you still have not personally witnessed and confirmed their existence. If you have witnessed and confirmed it yourself, then you will know this is not false.
chapterbody 62: If you only know that the “well” exists but do not know the existence of the “big sea,” it is a pity!
chapterbody 67: Eleven consciousness connotation – in Vasubandhu’s Commentary on the Mahayana-samgraha-sastra (Vasubandhu’s Mahayanasamgraha-bhasya).

chaptername: 038 Do Not Go to the Seaside!
chapterbody: ne year, a disciple by the surname Tao, brought his wife to see me.
chapterbody 2: Disciple Tao ran a construction business and was quite well-known. He was a devoted disciple.
chapterbody 4: Every time he came to see me, he always brought some souvenirs such as grape wines, biscuits, or local specialties. On the one hand, they were for offering to Grand Master Lu, and on the other hand, they were for offering to all the venerable on the shrine. He never came empty-handed.
chapterbody 6: Disciple Tao always asked for some tips for cultivation, for example:
chapterbody 8: “What is the sensation when the expedient energy (qi) is converted to wisdom energy (qi)?”
chapterbody 10: “What is the pith instruction for the energy (qi) of left and right channels entering into the central channel?”
chapterbody 12: “What is the pith instruction for descending and spreading the light drops?”
chapterbody 14: I would briefly and concisely tell disciple Tao the pith instructions and the main points.
chapterbody 16: This time, disciple Tao also brought his wife. Disciple Tao’s wife was very young. She had no religious belief, was an atheist, and her beauty was that of glamor.
chapterbody 18: Every time disciple Tao attended ceremonies, he was always alone by himself. (It was because his wife had no interest.)
chapterbody 20: This time she came along because disciple Tao had persuaded her several times, and she then reluctantly agreed. She was dressed head-to-toe in name brand clothing. She faced the shrine, but did not even want to put her palms together.
chapterbody 22: When she saw disciple Tao pay homage to me with full-body prostrations, she displayed a bit of disdain. Disciple Tao discussed buddhadharma with me, but she was indifferent and her expression was stiff.
chapterbody 23: She only watched the cars on the street outside that were speeding in both directions. She returned to her seat and gazed at me to size me up for a while.
chapterbody 25: She felt bored, and looked at the “Chopard” watch on her hand.
chapterbody 26: When disciple Tao was about to leave, she suddenly asked me, “I heard people say that your divination is very accurate, is that true or not?”
chapterbody 28: “There are always rumors,” I said.
chapterbody 30: “Can you look at my fate?”
chapterbody 32: “Very good.”
chapterbody 34: “Is there anything I need to be concerned with?” She asked.
chapterbody 36: I stabilized my mind for a moment; once my eyes closed, I saw “Poseidon,” the sea god in Greek mythology.
chapterbody 38: I was quite astonished.
chapterbody 40: I said to Mrs. Tao, “Do not go to the seaside!”
chapterbody 42: Mrs. Tao replied to me, “Do not worry! I do not know how to swim, and do not go to the beach to swim. When traveling abroad, any water activities such as motorized water sports, water skiing, scuba diving, glass-bottom boating, parasailing, or banana boating…. I would never take part in.
chapterbody 43: “Even at high-class clubs, I would only wear the swimsuit and sun-tan at the side of the swimming pool. I won’t actually enter the water. I have no affinity with water.”
chapterbody 45: Mrs. Tao then said, “Grand Master Lu, did you miscalculate?”
chapterbody 47: I replied, “Perhaps!”
chapterbody 49: But, I still advised her, “Do not go to the seaside!”
chapterbody 51: Disciple Tao together with his wife, drove away in his luxurious car.
chapterbody 52: One year later, Mrs. Tao forgot the words that I told her.
chapterbody 54: Everyone was standing on the rocks along the seaside to take pictures.
chapterbody 56: That day, strong gusty winds of level eleven bellowed.
chapterbody 57: Mrs. Tao wanted to have a picture by herself. She chose a piece of high reef. The ocean spray shot up from behind and exploded just like fireworks. Mrs. Tao had her back towards the sea to have her picture taken.
chapterbody 59: A strong gusty wind swept across. The waves became huge.
chapterbody 61: All of a sudden, a big wave rolled in and receded, and Mrs. Tao was drawn into the sea. In only an instant, her body was not seen floating up. Her body had already sunk to the bottom of the sea.
chapterbody 62: No amount of dredging could find her!
chapterbody 63: Once, she followed a tour to a scenic district of a certain country.
chapterbody 68: Oh Heavens! Disciple Tao cried his eyes out, and requested me to perform a bardo deliverance for her.
chapterbody 69: Disciple Tao told me, “She had forgotten Grand Master Lu’s warning of ‘do not go to the seaside!’”
chapterbody 71: The great teacher of the universal consciousness, able to carry out the path of loving-kindness; able to carry out the path of loving-kindness, able to carry out the path of great loving-kindness; the one who is able to carry out the path of loving-kindness of the Shakya clan, auspiciousness and perfection.

chaptername: 039 One Scene from “Bowl of Rice Cake Shop (Dian Kou Wan Guo)”
chapterbody: t the end of November 2011, in an entourage of disciples that included Master Lianning, Master Liandian, Lianya, and Lianzeng, we ate rice cakes at the “Bowl of Rice Cake Shop” in the neighborhood of the National Taiwan Museum of Fine Arts in Taichung. The rice cakes of this Bowl of Rice Cake Shop were very popular, and every afternoon, it would always be packed with people. Most of the time, people had to make reservations before coming to have the rice cake!
chapterbody 3: Hung on the wall of the Bowl of Rice Cake Shop were a painting and a calligraphy couplet that I admired. The painting in the center was Samantabhadra Bodhisattva riding on an elephant. The couplet, with one verse on each side of the painting, was written by the calligrapher, Lu Foting.
chapterbody 5: The second verse of the couplet read, “Able in loving-kindness is my master.”
chapterbody 7: I asked, “Who is the Able in loving-kindness?”
chapterbody 8: Lianning, Liandian, Lianya, Liandeng, and the rest, looked at one another, speechless.
chapterbody 10: I said, “Able in loving-kindness is Shakyamuni Buddha.”
chapterbody 11: I explained:
chapterbody 13: We chant the mantra of Shakyamuni Buddha:
chapterbody 15: “Om, mou-ni, mou-ni, ma-ha-mou-ni, shi-jia-mou-ni, so-ha.”
chapterbody 16: “Mou-ni” in this mantra represents “Able in loving-kindness.”
chapterbody 18: The explanation of the mantra is as follows:
chapterbody 20: “The great teacher of the universal consciousness, able to carry out the path of loving-kindness; able to carry out the path of loving-kindness, able to carry out the path of great loving-kindness; the one who is able to carry out the path of loving-kindness of the Shakya clan, auspiciousness and perfection.”
chapterbody 22: Therefore I said, “Able in loving-kindness is Shakyamuni Buddha.”
chapterbody 24: While I was explaining, two ladies came in through the entrance of the shop. Both of them waved at me, and said, “We are your disciples!”
chapterbody 26: One of the disciples said, “About several months ago, we met while Grand Master Lu and fellow disciples were eating rice cakes at ‘Bowl of Rice Cake Shop.’ I kneeled down and prayed for Grand Master’s blessing. It was because I had been married for eleven years, but I could not get pregnant. I had seen lots of famous doctors of Traditional Chinese and Western medicines, but the results were still negative.
