Knowing that Grand Master Lu has great dharma power, I therefore prayed for great blessing.”
chapterbody 28: The disciple continued saying, “At that time, Grand Master Lu stood up to bless me on the head. Through Grand Master’s palm on my top of my head, I could feel the dharma stream coursing down my whole body continuously without stopping, there was an expanse of brightness in front of my eyes. Subsequently, after I went home, I became pregnant. I am truly thankful to Grand Master Lu. Now, I am praying for a safe delivery for me and my child.”
chapterbody 30: I finally remembered:
chapterbody 32: This lady disciple, several months ago, had kneeled in front of me, praying for “pregnancy” blessing. The reason was, she had been married for eleven years but was infertile.
chapterbody 34: I closed my eyes, and saw the Samantabhadra Bodhisattva on the wall radiate light which entered into the womb of the lady disciple.
chapterbody 35: Wow! The response in my hand was very strong. That kind of vibration power was generated from the union of the Bodhisattva with my hand.
chapterbody 36: Inside the light that entered into the womb of the lady disciple, there was a small child.
chapterbody 38: At last, a few months later. She was pregnant!
chapterbody 40: I told the lady disciple, “This child will be a great sage in the future!”
chapterbody 42: Everyone in the Bowl of Rice Cake Shop saw this scene.
chapterbody 44: And:
chapterbody 46: There was a disciple who lived very far away overseas; she had been infertile for twenty years. Twenty years, plus twenty years [of age] before marriage—she was already forty years of age; would it be possible for her to give birth?
chapterbody 48: She did not give up. She prayed to the Padmakumara statue and prepared the greatest offerings. That night, she saw Padmakumara dressed in white robe, extraordinarily dignified, and wearing a Five Buddha Crown. There were five colors of precious jewels on the crown. Amongst them, a red precious jewel fell onto the hand of Padmakumara that immediately transformed into an exquisite cute little baby.
chapterbody 50: Padmakumara said to the lady disciple, “This baby is given to you!”
chapterbody 51: In her dream, her belly became large. When she woke up, she told her husband that she had a baby in her belly.
chapterbody 52: Her husband replied, “Thoughts in the daytime may get reflected in dreams at night. How can dreams be accurate?”
chapterbody 54: Now, her parents-in-law all took refuge, and so did her relatives and friends. Many people took refuge. All just because one dream led to a pregnancy!
chapterbody 63: But, since that day, the lady disciple really became pregnant.

chaptername: 040 I Will Take You to Fly
chapterbody: n Saturday, December 10, 2011, I was in Taiwan Lei Tsang Temple presiding over the “Treasure Source Tara” homa ceremony. On that day, over ten thousand people attended and listened to my exposition of the origination of Treasure Source Tara.
chapterbody 3: After the dharma talk, I also bestowed the empowerment of “Treasure Source Tara” to the crowd. During the empowerment, I had a special sensation.
chapterbody 5: Treasure Source Tara appeared in gold from the space; her appearance was similar to “Green Tara.” She flew freely above the empowerment banner and her flying postures were not like a flying bird, but rather in various standing or sitting postures. One hand formed the wish-granting mudra and the other hand was holding an “Utpala flower.” Different kinds of light emanated from her ten finger tips and the Utpala flower.
chapterbody 7: Within the lights were lots of treasures:
chapterbody 9: Lotus flowers
chapterbody 10: Jeweled carts
chapterbody 11: Golden lamps
chapterbody 12: Auspicious fruits
chapterbody 13: Incense oils (Vilepana)
chapterbody 14: Dharma wheels
chapterbody 15: Water with eight merits
chapterbody 16: Fragrant flowers
chapterbody 17: Buddha-fruit
chapterbody 18: Sublime bliss
chapterbody 19: Flying shoes
chapterbody 20: Etc…
chapterbody 22: Limitless seven treasures, eight treasures, twenty-one treasures, thirty-two treasures, and thirty-seven treasures, continuously descended from the space onto all disciples; even I, Living Buddha Sheng-yen Lu, also received one treasure, “Flying shoes.” I picked them up, and kept them close to my chest.
chapterbody 24: Treasure Source Tara asked me, “Grand Master Lu, you already can fly, what is the benefit of having the flying shoes?”
chapterbody 26: I replied, “I will bring someone with me to fly.”
chapterbody 28: Treasure Source Tara asked, “Fly to where?”
chapterbody 30: I replied, “Fly to the pure land of Maha Twin Lotus Ponds.”
chapterbody 32: Treasure Source Tara said, “Why not fly to the pure land of Samantabhadra Buddha Mother, which is the origin of Treasure Source Tara.”
chapterbody 34: I answered, “Buddha pure lands are all interconnected, I certainly will make offerings to the pure land of Samantabhadra Buddha Mother.”
chapterbody 36: Treasure Source Tara said, “Excellent! Excellent!”
chapterbody 38: After the ceremony, a disciple asked me, “Grand Master Lu, I heard that you could spiritually travel to the Buddha worlds of the ten directions, right?”
chapterbody 40: I replied, “I can fly.”
chapterbody 42: Disciple asked, “Can you take me to fly?”
chapterbody 44: I suddenly remembered that in my chest I had the “flying shoes” bestowed by Treasure Source Tara. I thought, what a coincidence that a disciple had asked.
chapterbody 46: I replied, “I will take you to fly!”
chapterbody 48: Disciple asked, “When?”
chapterbody 50: I answered, “Tonight.”
chapterbody 53: “Last night, I really flew. Grand Master Lu took me to fly. We flew over a big mountain and a big sea. We flew across the starry sky, and to Maha Twin Lotus Ponds to see all the Padmakumaras of different colors.
chapterbody 55: That kind of flying was actually very unusual. As long as both arms were stretched upwards and the feet were stepped off [from the ground], then one could fly comfortably. The body was like a falling leaf, as if in an illusion or in a dream.”
chapterbody 58: I said, “Of course it was real.”
chapterbody 63: And then we flew into the center of the lotus pistil of a lotus flower in Maha Twin Lotus Ponds, and flew to the pure land of Samantabhadra Buddha Mother. We saw Samantabhadra Buddha Mother, Nairatmya, Vajravarahi, White Tara, Green Tara and all the Buddha Mothers.
chapterbody 64: He said, “Grand Master Lu, you took me to fly, it was so real!”
chapterbody 67: The next day, December 11, 2011, I was at “Gan Lu Ching Tze Temple” conducting “Yogacara Ulka-mukha.” That disciple also attended, and he told me:

chapterbody: n December 10, 2011, after the “Treasure Source Tara” homa (fire offering) ceremony was completed at around six o’clock in the evening, I went to have my dinner at the dining hall of Taiwan Lei Tsang Temple.
chapterbody 2: A Mr. Cao Cangming was sitting next to me, at my right.
chapterbody 4: He said, “I knew Grand Master Lu over thirty years ago.”
chapterbody 6: I was startled. I asked, “How old were you at that time?”
chapterbody 8: He answered, “At that time, I was studying primary school.” (Dharma brother was now over forty years old.)
chapterbody 10: I asked, “How did you know me while studying primary school?”
chapterbody 12: He answered, “My mother brought me to see you to ask about my father. The location was at Jinhua Road. I felt that Grand Master Lu could really tell jokes. When adults listened to you, they laughed heartily!”
chapterbody 14: He said, “During that time, my father, who was serving at the police force, was the head of the local police station near Deji Reservoir. He and Grand Master Lu’s father, Lu Ershun, had frequent contact with each other. My father frequently chatted with Grand Master’s father. They were very close. And also because of this, my father and mother were able to visit Grand Master Lu.”
chapterbody 16: He said, “Grand Master Lu’s divination is really accurate!”
chapterbody 18: I asked, “How accurate is it?”
chapterbody 20: He answered, “My father was investigating a criminal case, and came seeking Grand Master Lu for instructions. Grand Master Lu had said, at a certain time and month, he would be able to capture this criminal by going to Yanchao Township in Kaohsiung City.”
chapterbody 22: “The result?”
chapterbody 24: He said, “The result was, he really caught the criminal! Due to this achievement, my father was transferred from Taichung County to Taichung City. Eventually, my father became a Senior Inspector and he remained so until he retired.”
chapterbody 26: He also said, “Grand Master Lu’s divination for my uncle is the most accurate.”
chapterbody 28: “How so?”
chapterbody 30: He said, “Grand Master Lu asserted that my uncle could become the Chief Inspector in the police force, and my uncle really did become the Chief Inspector. Grandmaster also told my uncle to be careful at fifty years old.”
chapterbody 32: “The result?”
chapterbody 34: He said, “At age fifty, he unexpectedly died of a heart attack!”
chapterbody 36: I asked, “I did not say that he would die, did I?”
chapterbody 38: He said, “You did not. Grand Master Lu only said, to be careful at age fifty.”
chapterbody 40: He also said, “When my uncle became Chief Inspector, and died at age fifty of a heart attack, it was considered quite sensational. The news media even published it.”
chapterbody 42: Cao Cangming said, “At that time, Grand Master Lu was at Taichung giving consultations. It created quite a stir. You were surrounded by many people everyday. My whole family came to ask Grand Master Lu for guidance—my father, mother, aunt, and uncle. The most accurate was the capture of the criminal, and the death of my uncle at age fifty.
chapterbody 43: Even the time, place, and age, was without deviation.”
chapterbody 45: I asked, “Has your father ever come to Taiwan Lei Tsang Temple?”
chapterbody 47: “He came before. He had always wanted to acknowledge the old relationship with Grand Master Lu’s father. It was just that your father, maybe, had forgotten him. When he knew old Mr. Lu Ershun had passed away, my father, in those days, was grief-stricken. He was continuously silent and not speaking!”
chapterbody 49: “Alas! Time passes really quickly! Hard to imagine that I am already sixty-seven years old, and you are also over forty. When I was giving consultation, I was only over twenty years old, and you were only in primary school.”
chapterbody 51: There is one saying:
chapterbody 52: “Human life is a live broadcast. Furthermore, there are no NGs”
chapterbody 56: Buddhadharma does not part from secular dharma. This is how it is!
chapterbody 57: Sheng-yen Lu

chapterbody: here was once a Buddhist layperson who enjoyed a great reputation in the Buddhist community. He knew both Vajrayana and Sutrayana very well. This great layperson was also a rich and powerful person. He donated money to build temples, to print sutras, to offer to the Sangha, and to do good deeds. In the religious community, everyone knew about him.
chapterbody 3: When he was living abroad in New York, he printed a sutra. Very few people know about this sutra. It was not widely circulated. The sutra is called Lotus Leaf Child Sutra. I came across this sutra by accident.
chapterbody 4: In the sutra, it teaches people that offering to the “Lotus Leaf Child” has great benefits:
chapterbody 6: 1. Increase in fortune.
chapterbody 7: 2. Improvement in wisdom.
chapterbody 8: 3. Great power and prestige.
chapterbody 9: 4. Great supernatural powers.
chapterbody 10: 5. Extension of life.
chapterbody 12: The statue of “Lotus Leaf Child” can be made using wood, porcelain, glass, clay, or stone. A model of a child standing on a lotus leaf is molded; the hair is tied into two buns on top of the head. The child can be male or female.
chapterbody 14: One head, two arms. The two hands, each extending an index finger. One finger pointing at the earth, the other pointing to the heavens.
chapterbody 15: (Similar to the child statue of Shakyamuni Buddha of the Buddha Bathing Festival.)
chapterbody 17: The difference between this statue and that of the child statue of Shakyamuni Buddha is the standing on the lotus leaf and also the two hair buns on top of the head.
chapterbody 19: The offering sadhana in the sutra is:
chapterbody 21: Offering is to be done in a private room. (Chickens, dogs, cats, and the like, are not allowed to enter.)
chapterbody 23: Offerings are flowers, incense, light, tea, fruits, children’s toys, and candies, etc.
chapterbody 25: Incense is to be lit three times a day: morning, noon, and night.
chapterbody 27: Cultivation practice is to be done in the afternoon.
chapterbody 29: Visualize “Lotus Leaf Child.”
chapterbody 31: Chant: (Mudra: one hand pointing to the heaven, one hand pointing to the earth.)
chapterbody 33: “Heaven Spirit, Earth Spirit, Lotus Leaf Child Spirit. Ji-ji-lu-ling. Seh.” (The more the better)
chapterbody 35: Entering of the Principal Deity into Oneself (Ru-wo) and Release of Oneself into the Cosmic Consciousness (Wo-ru). (Similar to entering samadhi.)

chapterbody 2: ne True Buddha School disciple, “Lianhua Changyu,” who was about fifty years old, and his wife, Mrs. Chen Ting, were invited to participate in a pilgrimage group of a nearby temple. They travel to an ancestral shrine for their pilgrimage.
chapterbody 4: The pilgrimage is a popular custom in Taiwan. For instance, every year, the Goddess Mazu is lifted in a sedan chair at Zhenlan Palace in Dajia to Fengtian Palace in Xingang.
chapterbody 6: This temple event can be considered number one in the whole province. They are number one in having the most people, welcoming Mazu, receiving Mazu, participating in the procession, burning incense, etc...
chapterbody 8: In Dajia’s Mazu Parade, besides Mazu, there are still Qian Li Yan (the General of Thousand Mile Sight), Shun Feng Er (the General of Brilliant Hearing), and various deities following. The leaders of the parade are quite many.
chapterbody 10: Central senior officials, regional senior officials, and a very large number of worshippers, stir up a commotion along the way. It can be said that this is a very grand occasion for the entire country.
chapterbody 12: This Mazu Mania of Taiwan during the third lunar month is not a small affair.
chapterbody 14: As for:
chapterbody 16: It goes without saying that there are so many temples of all sizes in the whole province of Taiwan, as well as deities, that it is practically impossible to count them all.
chapterbody 18: With the buses of the pilgrimage groups going here and there, tides of people going to the temple event, and the Ba Jia Jiang (bodyguards and attendants), Taiwan has become a believer of a diverse array of religious customs.
chapterbody 20: The pilgrimage group that “Lianhua Changyu” and his wife participated in was scheduled to start their journey.
chapterbody 22: On the night before they were to start their journey, they each had a dream.
chapterbody 24: Grand Master Lu appeared in their dream, and told them, “Do not go tomorrow!”
chapterbody 26: “Why?”
chapterbody 28: “There will be a motor vehicle accident!”
chapterbody 30: “In the vehicle are statues of Third Prince, Mazu, City God, Wang Ye, etc… Many deities are on the vehicle protecting it. There should not be anything happening.”
chapterbody 32: Grand Master Lu said, “The calamity is inevitable!”
chapterbody 34: The husband and wife saw Grand Master Lu’s warning in their dream at the same time. As a result, they were hesitating and could not decide on whether or not to go.
chapterbody 36: However, Lianhua Changyu was one of the group leaders, and also one of the leaders of the vicinity. If the leader were to play truant, how was he going to explain; the pilgrimage group was also suggested by the vicinity’s elders. If they do not go, they would be letting down these elders.
chapterbody 38: Cancelling was also not possible.
chapterbody 40: They could only summon up courage and participate. They were thinking that it was only a dream and dreams were not necessarily real.
chapterbody 42: At the end, their only solution was to wear a “Padmakumara” protection amulet on their bodies. The pilgrimage group set off as planned.
chapterbody 44: The whole bus journey went without a hitch. There were altogether three buses following one another. Lianhua Changyu was originally riding on the first bus, but was later switched to ride on the second bus. The pilgrimage also went very smoothly.
chapterbody 46: On their return journey, at Alishan, while they were on the turning point at the mid-height of the mountain, the brakes of the first bus suddenly failed.
chapterbody 48: As a result, the bus crashed against the road barrier and overturned. The impact caused it to dash out towards the slope. The car laid on its side at the roadside.
chapterbody 50: Heavens! It really was a motor vehicle accident!
chapterbody 53: Of the worshippers on the bus, one person died and seventeen people suffered severe injuries. They were admitted to the local hospital.
chapterbody 54: Finally—
chapterbody 56: Lianhua Changyu said:
chapterbody 57: He was supposed to ride on the first bus. On the return trip, he boarded the first bus, and found that his seat was already taken by another person. The person sitting there had said, “No need to change seats, just sit in my seat on the second bus.” Subsequently, Lianhua Changyu and even his wife, were then allocated to the second bus.
chapterbody 59: The problem was, the person who had switched seats with Lianhua Changyu, was the very one person who died in the motor vehicle accident.
chapterbody 61: Heavens, how could this be?
chapterbody 62: Was it fate?
chapterbody 65: Had Lianhua Changyu not switched seats, would he be the one who died?
chapterbody 67: The police and ambulance all arrived!
chapterbody 68: Was it the effect of the protection amulet?
chapterbody 69: Lianhua Changyu said, “When the pilgrimage group was on the road on Alishan, while spiraling downwards, we smelled waves of incense fragrance. Was it Padmakumara protecting? But, why did he not protect the people on the first bus?”

chapterbody 39: Some disciples, who although have taken refuge in me, still have inherent bad habits. These bad habits are not completely gotten rid of. Even people who are trying to gradually get rid of their bad habits, still have natures of greed, anger, and ignorance. They are not able to supersede the ordinary and enter into the sanctified state [of being] in such a short time.
chapterbody 41: Even though I am patient, they are not listening.

chapterbody 45: I always emphasize:

chapterbody 49: All [activities are done] under the tenets of humanity, rationality, and laws, to propagate the Buddha’s compassion and wisdom.
chapterbody 51: Disciples who have taken refuge need to abide by the laws.
chapterbody 52: Disciples who have taken refuge need to abide by the Vajrayana precepts. Not only abide by the “Five Precepts,” “Eight Precepts,” “Bodhisattva Vows,” “Bhiksu Precepts,” “Bhiksuni Precepts,” but even the “The Fifty Stanzas of Guru Devotion,” “The Fourteen Vajrayana Root Precepts,” and so on.
chapterbody 55: Our True Buddha School places emphasis on precepts. If precepts are broken, one must properly repent until one sees “the manifestations of repentance.” To acknowledge and correct one’s actions, thereupon, is the greatest blessing!

chapterbody 7: Disciple would forever not forget the 15th day of the 8th lunar month of this year, which was also the day of the Mid-Autumn Festival. My daughter Lianhua Xinyue’s headache had suddenly intensified and we immediately sent her to the hospital to do a series of tests. After almost three weeks later, no causes could be found.
chapterbody 9: My child would have severe headaches throughout the day; at night, the pain would worsen to the point that she would bash her own head. As she was unable to sleep, her body became weak and emaciated. Her eyes became dull, without life, and her face took on a pale and green tinge. The tormenting pain grinded her mercilessly and we were powerless to help the pitiful child. I bewailed my inability to protect her as a mother. I would be willing to lay my life down if it meant saving her!
chapterbody 11: After going through many setbacks, at last, with the help of the buddhas and bodhisattvas, we were lucky to get in touch with dharma sister Chan of Vancouver. With dharma sister Chan’s valuable assistance and arrangement, we became primary supplicants of homa offerings. By October 2, we were finally able to personally deliver our letter and photograph to Grand Master, beseeching his blessing.
chapterbody 13: I only saw Grand Master say softly, “Avalokitesvara Bodhisattva will be in charge.” Then he took my child’s photograph and pressed it on his forehead. This seemed like a casual gesture, but to my child it had an incomparably significant and profound meaning. An endless stream of powerful and invisible super dharma blessed her. After that, my daughter’s headache symptom finally disappeared.
chapterbody 15: As I watched her carrying her backpack and excitedly head to school, her adorable face once again showed the smile which had been absent for a long time. How can this disciple not be grateful for Grand Master’s life-saving kindness? No words can express this feeling of gratitude!
chapterbody 17: Later, in the evening of the 17th of October, I dreamt and saw that Grand Master personally came to my house to bless it. In the dream, Grand Master was pinching disciple’s nose, and squeezing out a small amount of secretion. After that dream, when I blew my nose, a pile of dense phlegm-like substance was expelled. A few days later, I pleasantly discovered that the allergic rhinitis that had been plaguing me for many years had vanished without treatment!
chapterbody 19: Over and over again, the facts showed that Grand Master is Buddha! It is the truth with no deception! The pure, bright buddha light shines and blesses us, allowing us to escape suffering! Grand Master saved our child, and also saved our whole family!
chapterbody 21: How extraordinary is Grand Master’s blessing power! As a True Buddha disciple, I am fortunate. I am unreservedly proud and blessed to be able to take refuge in Buddha Master!
chapterbody 23: With Grand Master’s rescue, I am even more aware how frightful cause and effect is! Human life is suffering, life is frail and momentary!
chapterbody 24: If one does not follow Grand Master’s teachings and seriously cultivate and seek the fundamental way to liberation, one is squandering human life massively!
chapterbody 26: Disciple, Lianhua Yuanyuan, again with daughter, Lianhua Xinyue, bow in deep gratitude to Grand Master!
chapterbody 28: Although I do not know how to express my feelings of gratitude to Grand Master with beautiful rhetoric and language, I am willing to wholeheartedly offer my action [body], speech, and mind to Grand Master. [I am willing] to follow Grand Master in cultivation life after life, dutifully undertake bodhi activities, and promote the Buddhadharma and save sentient beings within my meagre means!
chapterbody 30: I wish Buddha Master stay in good health, turn the great dharma wheel, succour all sentient beings, and not enter nirvana!
chapterbody 32: Disciple Lianhua Yuanyuan and family in prostration.
chapterbody 34: Om, gu-ru, lian-sheng, sid-dhi, hum.

chapterbody 49: Once, Grand Master Lu was giving me mo-ding. He patted the crown of my head two times. Usually he only pats once, but this time round, he did it twice. I reasoned that he patted me the second time because I did not stand up right away after the first one!

chapterbody 51: That day, when I returned home to cultivate, I noticed it was different than before. A gush of pure energy spread from the crown of my head downwards. The whole body felt as if it was getting an electric shock; it was filled with the bliss of the dharma. I felt at ease, with a feeling of lightness.
chapterbody 52: This feeling of comfort and ease did not just take place during cultivation. Even after the cultivation session ended, it continued to be present, and there was no doubt about it. The emergence of this secret sensation was continuously present. It was really too wonderful for words!

chapterbody 58: During the mo-ding, I held out my mala beads for Grand Master Lu to touch. After returning home, I placed the mala beads on the shrine. When I turned off the lights, the shrine emitted rainbow brilliance.
chapterbody 60: When I looked again, I realize that the light was actually coming from the mala beads. The rays of light had many colors. This was something I had never seen before. Was there something wrong with my eyes? I looked [at the scenery] outside the window and everything was normal. Then I looked at the shrine again, and rainbow colors were still there. This rainbow brilliance remained for seven days before fading off.

chapterbody: n writing this book, [Open Your Mind], I hope to “generate faith” in everyone. This is the purpose of writing this book.
chapterbody 3: For me, “writing” and “cultivation” are the core themes of my life. It has always been this way without change, from my youth to the age of sixty-seven.
chapterbody 5: My life is all about Buddhadharma.
chapterbody 6: Buddha is beyond this mundane world, believe it!
chapterbody 7: About others:
chapterbody 9: “I do not have anything else!”
chapterbody 11: Buddhadharma starts from faith. That is why having authored two hundred and twenty-six books, I still write about “generating faith,” because “faith is the mother of all achievements and virtue.”
chapterbody 13: Over these past years, because my mind has been frank and unattached—not wanting anything and not being bothered by anything, I have entered a state of purity and ease. I feel very contented with my life, and feel that life should be just like this. The worries of the past have been wiped clean. Now, when I look upon this world, it is with an even incisive insight. After realizing my mind and seeing my Buddha-nature:

chapterbody 29: This book, is a “faith-generating” book. Hence, it is a book on an expedient method leading to the gate of cultivation. The way I see it, even though there are so many incidents of spiritual responses, I use the true stories of these spiritual responses to guide people to enter the state of the “correct faith” of the Buddhadharma.
chapterbody 31: The Five Aggregates are empty in nature.
chapterbody 33: Entering profound samadhi, one understands that all dharma is like emptiness.
chapterbody 35: I!
chapterbody 37: Although I know that all Buddha lands are “illusory,” I am able to, through pure marvelous actions, dignify Buddha lands.
chapterbody 39: Although I know that buddhas and bodhisattvas are also “illusory,” I am able to, through pure marvelous actions, dignify buddhas and bodhisattvas.
chapterbody 41: Although I know that human life is “illusory,” I am able to, through pure marvelous actions, beautify one’s life.
chapterbody 43: Although I know that cause and effect, reincarnation, and nirvana, are all “illusory,” I am able to, through purified marvelous actions, explain about them.
chapterbody 45: I already know:
chapterbody 47: All dharmas are uniformly void and tranquil, neither arising nor cessation. Birth, death and nirvana, ordinary beings and all buddhas, are but appearances of flowers in the sky. (This is the pinnacle of emptiness.)
chapterbody 49: But:
chapterbody 50: However, this kind of realization, is obtained from in-depth study and relentlessly practice of the Buddhadharma for forty years in the human realm. If human birth is not obtained as a result of causes and conditions; if an in-depth investigation of the Buddhadharma is not performed as a result of causes and conditions; if faith is not commenced as a result of causes and conditions; if yogic responses are not obtained as a result of causes and conditions; if supernatural abilities are not possible due to causes and conditions; if wisdom does not arise due to causes and conditions; How do I become liberated?
chapterbody 51: I use illusory means to commence a person’s firm belief. Otherwise, words cannot describe the emptiness and tranquility of Buddha-nature. In accordance to the differences among sentient beings, I expound different dharmas.
chapterbody 52: Nagarjuna’s Fundamental Verses on the Middle Way (The Mulamadhyamakakarika):
chapterbody 55: Those for whom emptiness is possible, for them everything is possible. Those for whom emptiness is not possible, for them everything is not possible.
chapterbody 56: There has never been a thing (dharma) that does not arise from causes and conditions. Therefore, all things (dharmas), are empty.
chapterbody 57: Shakyamuni Buddha is also known as “King of Emptiness Buddha.”
chapterbody 59: The Platform Sutra of the Sixth Patriarch has a few important phrases that are also like this:
chapterbody 61: The wonderful nature of worldly people is originally empty, and there is not a single dharma which can be obtained. The true emptiness of the self-nature is also like this.
chapterbody 62: To realize that the three bodies have no self-nature, is to understand the four wisdoms of Bodhi.
chapterbody 63: All things (dharmas) arising from causes and conditions, I say are simply emptiness, which are also false names, which is also the meaning of the Middle Way.
chapterbody 68: Your original nature, like the void, has nothing visible, is the correct view; while nothing that can be known, is true suchness.

chapterbody: he True Buddha Translation Teams (TBTTs) would like to express the highest honor and deepest gratitude to Living Buddha Lian-sheng Sheng-yen Lu and Master Lianxiang for their continuing support and guidance on the translation effort. Without their compassion, wisdom, blessings, and encouragement, this project would not have reached fruition.
chapterbody 3: In addition, we would like to acknowledge the diligent work put forth by the following volunteers on this project: the lead¬er of US Daden, Master Lianseng; Monita Chan (translator), Janet Ho and Kai Chung Ho (editors), Jessie Loh (proofreader), and Renee Cordsen (publication). We would like to thank these dedicated and selfless volunteers who have contributed their time and effort to promote the works of Living Buddha Lian-sheng, and to support the publications of US Daden Culture.
chapterbody 5: We would also like to extend our sincere appreciation to all of the other volunteers who work behind the scenes, facilitating the transla¬tion process, and handling administrative responsibilities.
chapterbody 7: May all volunteers be blessed with immeasurable merits. May all sentient beings benefit from the ocean of wisdom.
